Try to update it w/ code reuse and not duplication.

396	// sessionMarkdown returns the session as markdown for the reader — the real specstory
397	// render when the session can be re-parsed (needs a cwd), else the plain FTS body.
398	func (m searchTUI) sessionMarkdown(s *sessionindex.Session) string {
399		if s.OriginCwd != "" {
400			if prov, err := m.registry.Get(s.Agent); err == nil {
401				if full, err := prov.GetAgentChatSession(s.OriginCwd, s.SessionID, false); err == nil &&
402					full != nil && full.SessionData != nil {
403					if md, err := session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession(full.SessionData, false, true); err == nil {
404						return md
405					}
406				}
407			}
408		}
409		if body, _ := m.store.SessionBody(s.Agent, s.SessionID); strings.TrimSpace(body) != "" {
410			return "```\n" + body + "\n```"
411		}
412		return "_(no readable content for this session)_"
413	}
414	
415	// runSearch performs the FTS query off the UI thread for the current scope.
416	func (m searchTUI) runSearch(seq int, ctx context.Context) tea.Cmd {
417		store := m.store
418		query := m.query
419		fq := ftsQuery(query)
420		scope := m.scopeProjectID
421		return func() tea.Msg {
422			if !queryReady(query) {
423				return searchFTSResultMsg{seq: seq}
424			}
425			sessions, _ := store.SearchContext(ctx, fq, scope)
426			return searchFTSResultMsg{seq: seq, sessions: sessions}
427		}
428	}
429	
430	func (m *searchTUI) applyResults(sessions []sessionindex.Session) {
431		m.results = nil
432		m.snippets = map[string]string{}
433		m.resultQuery = m.query
434		for _, s := range sessions {
435			if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
436				m.results = append(m.results, s)
437			}
438		}
439		if m.cursor > len(m.results)-1 {
440			m.cursor = len(m.results) - 1
441		}
442		if m.cursor < 0 {
443			m.cursor = 0
444		}
445		m.top = 0
446	}
447	
448	func (m *searchTUI) requestVisibleSnippets() tea.Cmd {
449		if !queryReady(m.resultQuery) || len(m.results) == 0 {
450			return nil
451		}
452		h := m.resultsHeight()
453		end := min(m.top+h, len(m.results))
454		if m.top >= end {
455			return nil
456		}
457		visible := append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.results[m.top:end]...)
458		query := ftsQuery(m.resultQuery)
459		store := m.store
460		m.snippetSeq++
461		seq := m.snippetSeq
462		return func() tea.Msg {
463			snips, _ := store.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), query, visible)
464			return searchSnippetResultMsg{seq: seq, snippets: snips}
465		}
466	}
467	
468	func searchFTSDebounce(seq int) tea.Cmd {
469		return tea.Tick(searchDebounceDelay, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
470			return searchFTSDebounceMsg{seq: seq}
471		})
472	}

497	func (m resumeTUI) loadPreview(s *sessionindex.Session) string {
498		body, err := m.store.SessionBody(s.Agent, s.SessionID)
499		if err != nil || strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {

504	
505	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
506	// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
507	// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
508	// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
509	// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
510	const minQueryLen = 2
511	
512	// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
513	// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
514	func queryReady(input string) bool {
515		n := 0
516		for _, r := range input {
517			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
518				n++
519				if n >= minQueryLen {
520					return true
521				}
522			}
523		}
524		return false
525	}
526	
527	func ftsQuery(input string) string {
528		var toks []string
529		for _, f := range strings.Fields(input) {
530			var b strings.Builder
531			for _, r := range f {
532				if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
533					b.WriteRune(r)
534				}
535			}
536			if b.Len() > 0 {
537				toks = append(toks, b.String()+"*")
538			}
539		}
540		return strings.Join(toks, " ")
541	}
542	
543	// ---- rendering ----
544	
545	var (
546		styDim    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
547		styFaint  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true)
548		styBold   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
549		styCursor = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("213")).Bold(true)
```

223	// updateList handles keys while browsing the session list.
224	func (m resumeTUI) updateList(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
225		switch msg.String() {
226		case "q", "ctrl+c":
227			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
228			return m, tea.Quit
229		case "esc":
230			// In a drilled-in project, esc returns to the browser; at home it quits.
231			if m.inBrowser {
232				m.enterBrowser()
233				return m, nil
234			}
235			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
236			return m, tea.Quit
237		case "tab":
238			// Toggle scope: home list ↔ all-projects browser.
239			m.enterBrowser()
240			return m, nil
241		case "up", "k":
242			m.moveCursor(-1)
243			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
244		case "down", "j":
245			m.moveCursor(1)
246			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
247		case "pgup":
248			m.moveCursor(-m.listHeight())
249			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
250		case "pgdown":
251			m.moveCursor(m.listHeight())
252			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
253		case "home", "g":
254			m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
255			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
256		case "end", "G":
257			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
258			m.clampScroll()
259			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
260		case "enter":
261			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
262				m.chosen = sel
263				m.mode = modeTarget
264				m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
265			}
266		case " ", "space":
267			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
268				m.previewBody = m.loadPreview(sel)
269				m.previewing = true
270			}
271		case "/":
272			m.searching = true
273			m.search.SetValue(m.searchQuery)
274			return m, m.search.Focus()
275		case "a":
276			return m, m.cycleAgent()
277		case "v":
278			m.toggleViewMode()
279			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
280		}
281		return m, nil
282	}

284	// updateSearch handles the full-text search input. The typed character is applied to the
285	// input immediately (instant feedback); the FTS query runs async + debounced so a slow
286	// query never blocks typing. See searchDebounceMsg / searchResultMsg.
287	func (m resumeTUI) updateSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
288		switch msg.String() {
289		case "esc":
290			m.searching = false
291			m.search.Blur()
292			m.searchQuery = ""
293			m.searchSeq++ // invalidate any in-flight search
294			m.snippetSeq++
295			m.applyFilter()
296			return m, nil
297		case "enter":
298			m.searching = false
299			m.search.Blur()
300			return m, nil
301		}
302		var cmd tea.Cmd
303		m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
304		m.searchQuery = m.search.Value()
305		m.searchSeq++
306		if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
307			// Too short to search: show the full (agent-filtered) list synchronously.
308			m.snippetSeq++
309			m.applyFilter()
310			return m, cmd
311		}
312		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeList))
313	}

751	func (m resumeTUI) renderPreview() string {

773	func (m resumeTUI) renderTarget() string {
774		var b strings.Builder
775		b.WriteString(styBold.Render("Resume into which agent?"))
776		if m.chosen != nil {
777			b.WriteString(styDim.Render("   " + sessionTitle(*m.chosen)))
778		}
779		b.WriteString("\n\n")
780		for i, a := range m.installed {
781			cursor := "   "
782			label := a.provider.Name()
783			if m.chosen != nil && a.id == m.chosen.Agent {
784				label += styFaint.Render(" (same agent — native resume)")
785			}
786			if i == m.targetCursor {
787				cursor = styCursor.Render(" ▸ ")
788				label = stySel.Render(a.provider.Name()) + label[len(a.provider.Name()):]
789			}
790			b.WriteString(cursor + label + "\n")
791		}
792		b.WriteString("\n")
793		b.WriteString(styDim.Render("↑↓ move   ↵ resume   esc back"))
794		return b.String()
795	}

814	// sessionTitle is the human label for a session: name, then slug, then short id.
815	func sessionTitle(s sessionindex.Session) string {
816		switch {
817		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Name) != "":
818			return s.Name
819		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Slug) != "":
820			return s.Slug
821		default:
822			return shortID(s.SessionID)
823		}
824	}
825	
826	// truncate shortens s to n runes with an ellipsis.
827	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
828		r := []rune(s)
829		if len(r) <= n {
830			return s
831		}
832		if n < 1 {
833			return ""
834		}
835		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
836	}
837	
838	// relativeTime renders an ISO 8601 timestamp as a compact "2m"/"3h"/"5d"/"Jun 2".
839	func relativeTime(iso string) string {
840		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
841		if err != nil {
842			return ""
843		}
844		d := time.Since(t)
845		switch {
846		case d < time.Minute:
847			return "now"
848		case d < time.Hour:
849			return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", int(d.Minutes()))
850		case d < 24*time.Hour:
851			return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", int(d.Hours()))
852		case d < 7*24*time.Hour:
853			return fmt.Sprintf("%dd", int(d.Hours()/24))
854		default:
855			lt := t.Local()
856			if lt.Year() == time.Now().Year() {
857				return lt.Format("Jan 2")
858			}
859			// Disambiguate prior years so "Dec 31" can't be mistaken for this year: "Dec 31 '25".
860			return lt.Format("Jan 2 '06")
861		}
862	}
863	
864	// previewText renders first user message · middle elision · final message to fit height.
865	func previewText(body string, width, height int) string {
866		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
867			return styFaint.Render("  (no preview available)")
868		}
869		if height < 4 {
870			height = 4
871		}
872		turns := strings.Split(body, "\n\n")
873		first := strings.TrimSpace(turns[0])
874		last := ""
875		if len(turns) > 1 {
876			last = strings.TrimSpace(turns[len(turns)-1])
877		}
878	
879		headBudget := height / 2
880		tailBudget := height - headBudget - 1
881	
882		var b strings.Builder
883		b.WriteString(styDim.Render("first ⟶ "))
884		b.WriteString("\n")
885		b.WriteString(clip(first, width, headBudget))
886		if last != "" {
887			b.WriteString("\n")
888			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("  ⋯ %d turns ⋯", len(turns))))
889			b.WriteString("\n")
890			b.WriteString(styDim.Render("final ⟶ "))
891			b.WriteString("\n")
892			b.WriteString(clip(last, width, tailBudget))
893		}
894		return b.String()
895	}
896	
897	// clip wraps/limits text to width columns and maxLines lines.
898	func clip(s string, width, maxLines int) string {
899		if maxLines < 1 {
900			maxLines = 1
901		}
902		var lines []string
903		for _, raw := range strings.Split(s, "\n") {
904			for len([]rune(raw)) > width {
905				r := []rune(raw)
906				lines = append(lines, string(r[:width]))
907				raw = string(r[width:])
908			}
909			lines = append(lines, raw)
910		}

127	func (m searchTUI) Init() tea.Cmd {
128		if m.seq > 0 {
129			// Pre-seeded query: the initial run isn't superseded yet, so a plain context is fine.
130			return tea.Batch(m.input.Focus(), m.runSearch(m.seq, context.Background()))
131		}
132		return m.input.Focus()
133	}
134	
135	func (m searchTUI) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
136		switch msg := msg.(type) {
137		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
138			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
139			m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
140			m.reader.SetHeight(m.readerHeight())
141			return m, nil
142		case searchFTSDebounceMsg:
143			if msg.seq == m.seq {
144				if m.searchCancel != nil {
145					m.searchCancel() // abort any prior in-flight query, freeing its connection
146				}
147				ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
148				m.searchCancel = cancel
149				return m, m.runSearch(msg.seq, ctx)
150			}
151			return m, nil
152		case searchFTSResultMsg:
153			if msg.seq == m.seq {
154				m.applyResults(msg.sessions)
155				m.loading = false // newest query answered; stale results leave loading set
156				return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets()
157			}
158			return m, nil
159		case searchSnippetResultMsg:
160			if msg.seq == m.snippetSeq {
161				if m.snippets == nil {
162					m.snippets = map[string]string{}
163				}
164				for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
165					m.snippets[key] = snip
166				}
167			}
168			return m, nil
169		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
170			switch m.screen {
171			case screenReader:
172				return m.updateReader(msg)
173			case screenTarget:
174				return m.updateTarget(msg)
175			default:
176				return m.updateResults(msg)
177			}
178		}
179		return m, nil
180	}
181	
182	// updateResults handles the always-on query input + result navigation.
183	func (m searchTUI) updateResults(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
184		switch msg.String() {
185		case "ctrl+c":
186			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
187			return m, tea.Quit
188		case "esc":
189			// Esc only clears the query; it never quits (the input is always focused, so the
190			// quit key has to be a non-printable one — ctrl+c). No-op when already empty.
191			if strings.TrimSpace(m.query) != "" {
192				m.query = ""
193				m.input.SetValue("")
194				m.seq++
195				m.snippetSeq++
196				m.results, m.snippets, m.resultQuery, m.cursor, m.top = nil, nil, "", 0, 0
197				m.loading = false
198			}
199			return m, nil
200		case "up", "ctrl+p":
201			m.moveCursor(-1)
202			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets()
203		case "down", "ctrl+n":
204			m.moveCursor(1)
205			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets()
206		case "pgup":
207			m.moveCursor(-m.resultsHeight())
208			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets()
209		case "pgdown":
210			m.moveCursor(m.resultsHeight())
211			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets()
212		case "enter", "right":
213			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
214				return m.openReader(sel)
215			}
216			return m, nil
217		case "ctrl+r":
218			// Resume the highlighted result without reading it first.
219			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil && len(m.installed) > 0 {
220				m.chosen = sel
221				m.screen = screenTarget
222				m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
223			}
224			return m, nil
225		case "tab":
226			return m, m.toggleScope()
227		case "ctrl+a":
228			return m, m.cycleAgent()
229		}
230	
231		// Anything else edits the query → instant input, debounced async search.
232		var cmd tea.Cmd
233		m.input, cmd = m.input.Update(msg)
234		m.query = m.input.Value()
235		m.seq++
236		if !queryReady(m.query) {
237			// Empty or too short to search: clear results, don't fire a query.
238			m.snippetSeq++
239			m.results, m.snippets, m.resultQuery, m.cursor, m.top = nil, nil, "", 0, 0
240			m.loading = false
241			return m, cmd
242		}
243		m.loading = true // show "Searching…" through the debounce + query
244		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchFTSDebounce(m.seq))
245	}
246	
247	func (m searchTUI) updateReader(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
248		switch msg.String() {
249		case "q", "ctrl+c":
250			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
251			return m, tea.Quit
252		case "esc", "left":
253			m.screen = screenResults
254			return m, nil
255		case "r":
256			if m.readerSession != nil && len(m.installed) > 0 {
257				m.chosen = m.readerSession
258				m.screen = screenTarget
259				m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
260			}
261			return m, nil
262		}
263		var cmd tea.Cmd
264		m.reader, cmd = m.reader.Update(msg)
265		return m, cmd
266	}
267	
268	func (m searchTUI) updateTarget(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
269		switch msg.String() {
270		case "esc":
271			// Back to wherever we came from (reader if open, else results).
272			if m.readerSession != nil {
273				m.screen = screenReader
274			} else {
275				m.screen = screenResults
276			}
277			return m, nil
278		case "ctrl+c":
279			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
280			return m, tea.Quit
281		case "up", "k":
282			if m.targetCursor > 0 {
283				m.targetCursor--
284			}
285		case "down", "j":
286			if m.targetCursor < len(m.installed)-1 {
287				m.targetCursor++
288			}
289		case "enter":
290			m.result = resumeTUIResult{session: m.chosen, targetID: m.installed[m.targetCursor].id}
291			return m, tea.Quit
292		}
293		return m, nil
294	}

296	// ---- mechanics ----
297	
298	func (m *searchTUI) moveCursor(delta int) {
299		if len(m.results) == 0 {
300			return
301		}
302		m.cursor += delta
303		if m.cursor < 0 {
304			m.cursor = 0
305		}
306		if m.cursor > len(m.results)-1 {
307			m.cursor = len(m.results) - 1
308		}
309		h := m.resultsHeight()
310		if m.cursor < m.top {
311			m.top = m.cursor
312		}
313		if m.cursor >= m.top+h {
314			m.top = m.cursor - h + 1
315		}
316		if m.top < 0 {
317			m.top = 0
318		}
319	}
320	
321	func (m searchTUI) selected() *sessionindex.Session {
322		if m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.results) {
323			return nil
324		}
325		return &m.results[m.cursor]
326	}
327	
328	func (m *searchTUI) toggleScope() tea.Cmd {
329		if !m.homeHasSessions {
330			return nil // nothing to scope to
331		}
332		if m.scopeProjectID == "" {
333			m.scopeProjectID = m.homeProjectID
334		} else {
335			m.scopeProjectID = ""
336		}
337		m.seq++
338		return m.rerunSearch()
339	}
340	
341	func (m *searchTUI) cycleAgent() tea.Cmd {
342		cur := 0
343		for i, id := range m.agentCycle {
344			if id == m.agentFilter {
345				cur = i
346				break
347			}
348		}
349		m.agentFilter = m.agentCycle[(cur+1)%len(m.agentCycle)]
350		return m.rerunSearch()
351	}
352	
353	// rerunSearch re-queries synchronously for the current scope (used by scope/agent toggles,
354	// which are one-off actions where a brief query is fine). applyResults applies the agent
355	// filter, so changing it re-filters via a fresh query.
356	func (m *searchTUI) rerunSearch() tea.Cmd {
357		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
358		m.loading = false // synchronous re-query: results are ready by the next render
359		if !queryReady(m.query) {
360			m.snippetSeq++
361			m.results, m.snippets, m.resultQuery = nil, nil, ""
362			return nil
363		}
364		if sessions, err := m.store.Search(ftsQuery(m.query), m.scopeProjectID); err == nil {
365			m.applyResults(sessions)
366		}
367		return m.requestVisibleSnippets()
368	}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-23 20:29:12Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-23 20:29:17Z)**_

This is a clear, well-scoped refactor. Here's my plan, then I'll implement it:

**What `search` does (the good version):** `openReader` → `renderGlamour(m.sessionMarkdown(s), m.width)` into a scrollable `viewport.Model`. `sessionMarkdown` does the *real* specstory render (re-parse via the provider when a cwd is known), falling back to the FTS body in a code fence. Glamour (the terminal-markdown library) styles it.

**What `resume` does (the "bunk" version):** `loadPreview` grabs the raw FTS body, and `previewText` shows a `first ⟶ … ⋯ N turns ⋯ … final ⟶` elision — no markdown rendering, no scrolling.

**The fix (reuse, no duplication):**
1. Promote `sessionMarkdown` to a package-level func `sessionMarkdown(registry, store, s)` so both TUIs share it (`renderGlamour` is already package-level).
2. Give `resumeTUI` a `viewport.Model` reader + `registry`, and render the preview exactly like search's reader.
3. Delete the now-dead `loadPreview`, `previewText`, `clip`, and `previewBody`.

Let me make the edits.

All green. Build passes, tests pass, lint is clean on both files.

## Summary

`specstory resume`'s preview now works exactly like `specstory search`'s reader: a **glamour-rendered, scrollable view of the real specstory markdown**, instead of the old `first ⟶ … ⋯ N turns ⋯ … final ⟶` elision over the raw FTS body.

**Shared (no duplication):**
- Promoted `searchTUI.sessionMarkdown` to a package-level `sessionMarkdown(registry, store, s)` in `search_tui.go` — the real re-parse-via-provider render with the FTS-body fence fallback. Both the search reader and the resume preview call it.
- `renderGlamour` was already package-level; both now use it.

**`resume_tui.go` changes:**
- `resumeTUI` gained a `registry *factory.Registry`, a `viewport.Model reader`, and `readerSession`; dropped `previewBody`.
- `newResumeTUI` takes the `registry` (passed through from `selectResumeViaTUI`, which already had it) and initializes the viewport.
- New `openPreview` mirrors search's `openReader` (set width/height, glamour content, `GotoTop`); `previewHeight` mirrors `readerHeight` (chrome = 4).
- `updatePreview` forwards `↑↓`/`pgup`/`pgdn` to the viewport and closes on space/esc/q/enter.
- `WindowSizeMsg` now resizes the reader.
- `renderPreview` renders the viewport with a `Preview · <agent> · <title>` header and a scroll-hint footer.
- **Deleted** the now-dead `loadPreview`, `previewText`, `clip`, and `chosenOrSelected`.

One small behavior note: I kept the trigger and overlay framing identical to before (open with `space` from the list, close with `space`/`esc`), just swapped the body for the glamour reader — so the rest of the resume UX is unchanged.

Want me to run the binary and open a preview to eyeball it, or are you good to try `./specstory resume` yourself?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-23 22:34:57Z)**_

Looking at your list, almost everything is a clean mechanical normalization. But two of your asks — **"`<space>` preview" in both** and **"search field at the bottom in both"** — collide with how `search` fundamentally works today, and I want your call before I touch it.

Here's the tension: `search` is **type-to-search-immediately** — the `/` input is always focused, so every keystroke edits the query (that's why `space` can't preview there, and why quit is `ctl-c` not `q`). `resume` is **modal** — you press `/` to search, and otherwise single keys navigate (`space` preview, `a`, `v`, `q`).

You can't have `space` = preview in `search` while typing also goes straight into a query — `space` is a valid search character. So to truly converge them, `search` has to become modal like `resume`. Your "make `ctl-q` quit search" ask, though, hints you might be picturing `search` staying type-to-search (you'd only need `ctl-q` if `q` types). So I want to confirm the direction.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-23 22:36:35Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**How should `search` handle typing, so it can converge with `resume`?**

Options:
- **Modal, like resume** - List shown by default; press / to search; single keys navigate (space=preview, a=agent, v=view, q=quit); input sits at the bottom. Fully converges the two UIs and gives you exactly your list. Trade-off: one extra keypress (/) before typing a query — you lose instant type-to-search.
- **Keep type-to-search** - search stays always-on input (typing filters instantly), just relocated to the bottom. Preview stays on Enter (can't be space). Quit is ctl-c / ctl-q. The two UIs stay slightly different on preview-key and quit-key, but search keeps its instant feel.

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

// CreateSearchCommand builds `specstory search [query…]` — the read-first sibling of
// resume. It opens an always-on search over the session index; `↵` reads a session
// (glamour-rendered), and `r` resumes it through the same launch path as `resume`.
// See docs/SESSION-SEARCH.md.
func CreateSearchCommand() *cobra.Command {
	return &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "search [query…]",
		Short: "Search and read your past coding-agent sessions",
		Long: `Full-text search across every session SpecStory has indexed, then read the match.

'search' opens an interactive search of your session history. Type to search, press enter to
read a session (rendered for the terminal), and press 'r' to resume it in an agent. Anything
after the command pre-seeds the query, e.g. 'specstory search max cpu'.`,
		Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			registry := factory.GetRegistry()
			initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))

			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}

			store, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()

			if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
				fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
				return nil
			}

			homeID, homeName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
			if idErr != nil || homeID == "" {
				homeID, homeName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
			}
			homeSessions, _ := store.ListByProject(homeID)
			homeHasSessions := len(homeSessions) > 0

			agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
			var installed []agentChoice
			for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
				prov, err := registry.Get(id)
				if err != nil {
					continue
				}
				agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
				if prov.Check("").Success {
					installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
				}
			}

			viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
			if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
				viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
				lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
			}

			model := newSearchTUI(store, registry, agents, installed, homeID, homeName, homeHasSessions, "", lastAgent, initialQuery)
			final, err := tea.NewProgram(model).Run()
			if err != nil {
				return fmt.Errorf("search failed: %w", err)
=========RESUME=========

// Best-effort UI writers. The destination is the user's terminal, so write
// errors are not actionable and are intentionally ignored.
func fprintf(w io.Writer, format string, a ...any) { _, _ = fmt.Fprintf(w, format, a...) }
func fprintln(w io.Writer, a ...any)               { _, _ = fmt.Fprintln(w, a...) }
func fprint(w io.Writer, a ...any)                 { _, _ = fmt.Fprint(w, a...) }

// resumePlan captures the user's choices from the interactive selection.
type resumePlan struct {
	from      spi.Provider
	fromID    string
	sessionID string
	// fromCwd is the directory the source session was originally launched from
	// (the index's origin_cwd). It can differ from the user's current cwd when a
	// session is picked from another project via the all-projects browser. The
	// source data load must use this, because some providers (e.g. Codex) locate a
	// session by matching its recorded cwd — looking under the current cwd would
	// find nothing. Empty for older index rows; callers fall back to the current cwd.
	fromCwd string
	to      spi.Provider
	toID    string
}

// agentChoice pairs a registry ID with its provider.
type agentChoice struct {
	id       string
	provider spi.Provider
}

'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.

Pass an agent to pre-select the resume target, e.g. 'specstory resume codex'.`

	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{

		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
			slog.Info("Running in resume mode")

			registry := factory.GetRegistry()

1257	// dateBucket groups a timestamp into a relative bucket for the browser rollup.
1258	func dateBucket(iso string) string {
1259		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1260		if err != nil {
1261			return "Older"
1262		}
1263		switch d := dayDiff(t.Local()); {
1264		case d <= 0:
1265			return "Today"
1266		case d == 1:
1267			return "Yesterday"
1268		case d < 7:
1269			return "Previous 7 days"
1270		case d < 30:
1271			return "Previous 30 days"
1272		default:
1273			return "Older"
1274		}
1275	}
1276	
1277	// dayDiff returns whole calendar days between now and t (both local), now - t.
1278	func dayDiff(t time.Time) int {
1279		now := time.Now()
1280		ny, nm, nd := now.Date()
1281		ty, tm, td := t.Date()
1282		a := time.Date(ny, nm, nd, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1283		c := time.Date(ty, tm, td, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1284		return int(a.Sub(c).Hours() / 24)
1285	}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-23 22:41:20Z)**_

Confirmed against the code. Your instinct is dead-on, and it's even cleaner than it looks: **`resumeTUI` is already a superset of `searchTUI`.** It already has an FTS-across-all-projects search (`globalActive`/`globalSearching` → `updateGlobalResults`) whose enter-jumps-to-target flow is functionally identical to `search`. `searchTUI` adds literally three things on top: always-on input, a default all-projects scope, and the "SpecStory Search" title. Nothing else.

So yes — one model, two entry points. Here's the shape:

## One TUI, two doors

The merged model (I'd rename `resumeTUI` → `sessionsTUI` since it now serves both). The **only** per-command differences:

| | `specstory resume [agent]` | `specstory search [query]` |
|---|---|---|
| **Start state** | session list, this project | global search, all projects, **input focused** |
| **Positional arg** | pre-select target agent | pre-seed the query |
| **Header title** | `SpecStory Resume` | `SpecStory Search` |

Everything else — keys, footers, header layout, project label, preview, the target-agent step, dense/sparse — is shared *because it's one model*. `search_tui.go` (~660 lines) gets deleted.

## This also dissolves the tension from before

Because `search` **starts with the input focused**, you still type immediately — the instant-search feel is preserved. `space` = preview doesn't conflict, because `space` only previews when you're *browsing results* (after `esc`/`enter` out of the input) — exactly like `resume` today. So we get "`<space>` preview" everywhere *and* keep search feeling instant. No `ctl` cuteness needed anywhere — it's modal, so single keys are free.

## Proposed unified keymap (no ctl)

```
↑↓ / j k   move          /        search
↵          resume        a        agent filter
<space>    preview       v        dense/sparse
tab        all projects  esc      back / clear
q          quit          (ctrl+c always hard-quits)
```

## The genuine micro-decisions (my rec in **bold**)

1. **Does `search` land in all-projects, or this-project?** → **All projects** (matches today's default; this-project search is still one `/` away inside the list). Keep `tab` to toggle scope.
2. **`enter` semantics** → **`enter` = resume, `space` = preview**, everywhere. (Drops search's old `enter`=read / `ctrl+r`=resume.)
3. **Rename `resumeTUI` → `sessionsTUI`?** → **Yes**, since it's no longer resume-specific. Low risk, all in one package.

The work: generalize the model's constructor + `Init` to accept a start-state and title, point both `search.go` and `resume.go` at it, delete `search_tui.go`, and apply your header/footer/label normalizations once.

Want me to go ahead on this — and do you agree with the three calls above? If you'd rather I formalize it as a written plan first, I can do that too.

134			reader:          viewport.New(),
135			projectID:       projectID,
136			projectName:     projectName,
137			homeProjectID:   projectID,
138			homeProjectName: projectName,
139			homeSessions:    sessions,
140			agents:          agents,
141			installed:       installed,
142			presetTo:        presetTo,
143			lastAgent:       lastAgent,
144			all:             sessions,
145			viewMode:        viewMode,
146			search:          ti,
147			projSearch:      pi,
148			globalInput:     gi,
149		}
150		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
151		m.applyFilter()
152		// Empty current project → open straight into the all-projects browser.
153		if len(sessions) == 0 {
154			m.startedInBrowser = true
155			m.enterBrowser()
156		}
157		return m
158	}
159	
160	func (m resumeTUI) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
161	
162	func (m resumeTUI) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
163		switch msg := msg.(type) {
164		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
165			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
166			m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
167			m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
168			return m, nil
169		case searchDebounceMsg:
170			// Fire the actual query only if no newer keystroke has arrived (debounce).
171			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
172				if m.searchCancel != nil {
173					m.searchCancel() // abort any prior in-flight query, freeing its connection
174				}
175				ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
176				m.searchCancel = cancel
177				return m, m.runSearch(msg.seq, msg.kind, ctx)
178			}
179			return m, nil
180		case searchResultMsg:
181			// Apply only the latest query's results (discard stale async results).
182			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
183				m.applySearchResults(msg.kind, msg.sessions)
184				return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(msg.kind)
185			}
186			return m, nil
187		case resumeSnippetResultMsg:
188			if msg.seq == m.snippetSeq {
189				if msg.kind == modeProjects {
190					if m.globalSnippets == nil {
191						m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
192					}
193					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
194						m.globalSnippets[key] = snip
195					}
196				} else {
197					if m.filteredSnippets == nil {
198						m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
199					}
200					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
201						m.filteredSnippets[key] = snip
202					}
203				}
204			}
205			return m, nil
206		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
207			switch {
208			case m.mode == modeTarget:
209				return m.updateTarget(msg)
210			case m.mode == modeProjects:
211				switch {
212				case m.globalSearching:
213					return m.updateGlobalSearch(msg)
214				case m.globalActive:
215					return m.updateGlobalResults(msg)
216				case m.projSearching:
217					return m.updateProjectSearch(msg)
218				default:
219					return m.updateProjects(msg)
220				}
221			case m.searching:
222				return m.updateSearch(msg)
223			case m.previewing:
224				return m.updatePreview(msg)
225			default:
226				return m.updateList(msg)
227			}
228		}
229		return m, nil
230	}
231	
232	// updateList handles keys while browsing the session list.
233	func (m resumeTUI) updateList(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
234		switch msg.String() {
235		case "q", "ctrl+c":
236			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
237			return m, tea.Quit
238		case "esc":
239			// In a drilled-in project, esc returns to the browser; at home it quits.
240			if m.inBrowser {
241				m.enterBrowser()
242				return m, nil
243			}
244			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
245			return m, tea.Quit
246		case "tab":
247			// Toggle scope: home list ↔ all-projects browser.
248			m.enterBrowser()
249			return m, nil
250		case "up", "k":
251			m.moveCursor(-1)
252			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
253		case "down", "j":
254			m.moveCursor(1)
255			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
256		case "pgup":
257			m.moveCursor(-m.listHeight())
258			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
259		case "pgdown":
260			m.moveCursor(m.listHeight())
261			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
262		case "home", "g":
263			m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
264			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
265		case "end", "G":
266			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
267			m.clampScroll()
268			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
269		case "enter":
270			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
271				m.chosen = sel
272				m.mode = modeTarget
273				m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
274			}
275		case " ", "space":
276			if sel := m.selected(); sel != nil {
277				return m.openPreview(sel)
278			}
279		case "/":
280			m.searching = true
281			m.search.SetValue(m.searchQuery)
282			return m, m.search.Focus()
283		case "a":
284			return m, m.cycleAgent()
285		case "v":
286			m.toggleViewMode()
287			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
288		}
289		return m, nil
290	}
291	
292	// updateSearch handles the full-text search input. The typed character is applied to the
293	// input immediately (instant feedback); the FTS query runs async + debounced so a slow
294	// query never blocks typing. See searchDebounceMsg / searchResultMsg.
295	func (m resumeTUI) updateSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
296		switch msg.String() {
297		case "esc":
298			m.searching = false
299			m.search.Blur()
300			m.searchQuery = ""
301			m.searchSeq++ // invalidate any in-flight search
302			m.snippetSeq++
303			m.applyFilter()
304			return m, nil
305		case "enter":
306			m.searching = false
307			m.search.Blur()
308			return m, nil
309		}
310		var cmd tea.Cmd
311		m.search, cmd = m.search.Update(msg)
312		m.searchQuery = m.search.Value()
313		m.searchSeq++
314		if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
315			// Too short to search: show the full (agent-filtered) list synchronously.
316			m.snippetSeq++
317			m.applyFilter()
318			return m, cmd
319		}
320		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeList))
321	}
322	
323	// updatePreview handles keys while the glamour preview reader is open. Close keys return
324	// to the list; everything else (↑↓ pgup/pgdn) scrolls the viewport, like search's reader.
325	func (m resumeTUI) updatePreview(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
326		switch msg.String() {
327		case " ", "space", "esc", "q", "enter":
328			m.previewing = false
329			m.readerSession = nil
330			return m, nil
331		}
332		var cmd tea.Cmd
333		m.reader, cmd = m.reader.Update(msg)
334		return m, cmd
335	}
336	
337	// openPreview loads the highlighted session into the glamour reader. Identical to search's
338	// openReader so the two previews render the same way.
339	func (m resumeTUI) openPreview(s *sessionindex.Session) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
340		m.readerSession = s
341		m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
342		m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
343		m.reader.SetContent(renderGlamour(sessionMarkdown(m.registry, m.store, s), m.width))
344		m.reader.GotoTop()
345		m.previewing = true
346		return m, nil
347	}
348	
349	// previewHeight is the viewport height inside the preview chrome (title + two rules + footer).
350	func (m resumeTUI) previewHeight() int {
351		const chrome = 4
352		h := m.height - chrome
353		if h < 1 {
354			h = 1
355		}
356		return h
357	}
358	
359	// updateTarget handles the target-agent selection step.
360	func (m resumeTUI) updateTarget(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
361		switch msg.String() {
362		case "esc", "q":
363			m.mode = modeList
364			m.chosen = nil
365		case "ctrl+c":
366			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
367			return m, tea.Quit
368		case "up", "k":
369			if m.targetCursor > 0 {
370				m.targetCursor--
371			}
372		case "down", "j":
373			if m.targetCursor < len(m.installed)-1 {
374				m.targetCursor++
375			}
376		case "enter":
377			m.result = resumeTUIResult{session: m.chosen, targetID: m.installed[m.targetCursor].id}
378			return m, tea.Quit
379		}
380		return m, nil
381	}
382	
383	// ---- list mechanics ----
384	
385	func (m *resumeTUI) moveCursor(delta int) {
386		if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
387			return
388		}
389		m.cursor += delta
390		if m.cursor < 0 {
391			m.cursor = 0
392		}
393		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
394			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
395		}
396		m.clampScroll()
397	}
398	
399	func (m *resumeTUI) clampScroll() {
400		h := m.listHeight()
401		if m.cursor < m.top {
402			m.top = m.cursor
403		}
404		if m.cursor >= m.top+h {
405			m.top = m.cursor - h + 1
406		}
407		if m.top < 0 {
408			m.top = 0
409		}
410	}
411	
412	func (m resumeTUI) selected() *sessionindex.Session {
413		if m.cursor < 0 || m.cursor >= len(m.filtered) {
414			return nil
415		}
416		return &m.filtered[m.cursor]
417	}
418	
419	// rowsPerSession is how many terminal lines one list row occupies in each view mode.
420	func (m resumeTUI) rowsPerSession() int {
421		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
422			return 2
423		}
424		return 1
425	}
426	
427	// listHeight is how many sessions fit in the list region (height minus chrome).
428	func (m resumeTUI) listHeight() int {
429		const chrome = 6 // header(2) + glance(1) + footer(2) + margins
430		avail := m.height - chrome
431		if avail < 1 {
432			avail = 1
433		}
434		n := avail / m.rowsPerSession()
435		if n < 1 {
436			n = 1
437		}
438		return n
439	}
440	
441	func (m *resumeTUI) rebuildAgentCycle() {
442		present := map[string]bool{}
443		for _, s := range m.all {
444			present[s.Agent] = true
445		}
446		ids := make([]string, 0, len(present))
447		for id := range present {
448			ids = append(ids, id)
449		}
450		sort.Strings(ids)
451		m.agentCycle = append([]string{""}, ids...)
452	}
453	
454	func (m *resumeTUI) cycleAgent() tea.Cmd {
455		cur := 0
456		for i, id := range m.agentCycle {
457			if id == m.agentFilter {
458				cur = i
459				break
460			}
461		}
462		m.agentFilter = m.agentCycle[(cur+1)%len(m.agentCycle)]
463		m.applyFilter()
464		return m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeList)
465	}
466	
467	func (m *resumeTUI) toggleViewMode() {
468		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
469			m.viewMode = "dense"
470		} else {
471			m.viewMode = "sparse"
472		}
473		m.clampScroll()
474	}
475	
476	// applyFilter rebuilds the visible list from the agent filter and search query. When a
477	// search is active, snippets are fetched lazily for the visible rows; otherwise the list
478	// is the project's sessions filtered by agent and snippets are cleared.
479	func (m *resumeTUI) applyFilter() {
480		m.filtered = m.filtered[:0]
481		m.filteredSnippets = nil
482	
483		if queryReady(m.searchQuery) {
484			var out []sessionindex.Session
485			if sessions, err := m.store.Search(ftsQuery(m.searchQuery), m.projectID); err == nil {
486				for _, s := range sessions {
487					if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
488						out = append(out, s)
489					}
490				}
491			}
492			m.filtered = out
493			m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
494		} else {
495			out := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, len(m.all))
496			for _, s := range m.all {
497				if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
498					out = append(out, s)
499				}
500			}
501			m.filtered = out
502		}
503	
504		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
505			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
506		}
507		if m.cursor < 0 {
508			m.cursor = 0
509		}
510		m.top = 0
511		m.clampScroll()
512	}
513	
514	func (m resumeTUI) defaultTargetIndex() int {
515		// Prefer the explicit preset (`resume <agent>`), else the last-resumed agent,
516		// else the chosen session's own agent (same-agent resume).
517		want := m.presetTo
518		if want == "" {
519			want = m.lastAgent
520		}
521		if want == "" && m.chosen != nil {
522			want = m.chosen.Agent
523		}
524		for i, a := range m.installed {
525			if a.id == want {
526				return i
527			}
528		}
529		return 0
530	}
531	
532	// ftsQuery turns free-form input into a safe FTS5 prefix query (alnum tokens only).
533	// minQueryLen is the shortest query we run a full-text search for. A single-character
534	// prefix (e.g. "t*") matches almost the entire corpus, and ranking + snippet generation
535	// over that set is pathologically slow (~80s on a ~800-session index), so we never fire
536	// it. Shared by resume and search so both behave the same.
537	const minQueryLen = 2
538	
539	// queryReady reports whether input has enough searchable characters (letters/digits, the
540	// same runes ftsQuery keeps) to run an FTS query. Below minQueryLen we don't search.
541	func queryReady(input string) bool {
542		n := 0
543		for _, r := range input {
544			if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
545				n++
546				if n >= minQueryLen {
547					return true
548				}
549			}
550		}
551		return false
552	}
553	
554	func ftsQuery(input string) string {
555		var toks []string
556		for _, f := range strings.Fields(input) {
557			var b strings.Builder
558			for _, r := range f {
559				if unicode.IsLetter(r) || unicode.IsDigit(r) {
560					b.WriteRune(r)
561				}
562			}
563			if b.Len() > 0 {
564				toks = append(toks, b.String()+"*")
565			}
566		}
567		return strings.Join(toks, " ")
568	}
569	
570	// ---- rendering ----
571	
572	var (
573		styDim    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("240"))
574		styFaint  = lipgloss.NewStyle().Faint(true)
575		styBold   = lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true)
576		styCursor = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("213")).Bold(true)
577		stySel    = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("255")).Bold(true)
578	)
579	
580	func (m resumeTUI) View() tea.View {
581		var content string
582		switch {
583		case m.mode == modeTarget:
584			content = m.renderTarget()
585		case m.mode == modeProjects && m.globalActive:
586			content = m.renderGlobalResults()
587		case m.mode == modeProjects:
588			content = m.renderProjects()
589		case m.previewing:
590			content = m.renderPreview()
591		default:
592			content = m.renderListScreen()
593		}
594		v := tea.NewView(content)
595		v.AltScreen = true
596		return v
597	}
598	
599	func (m resumeTUI) renderListScreen() string {
600		var b strings.Builder
601		b.WriteString(m.renderHeader())
602		b.WriteString("\n")
603		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
604		b.WriteString("\n")
605		b.WriteString(m.renderRows())
606		b.WriteString("\n")
607		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
608		b.WriteString("\n")
609		b.WriteString(m.renderGlance())
610		b.WriteString("\n")
611		b.WriteString(m.renderFooter())
612		return b.String()
613	}
614	
615	func (m resumeTUI) lineWidth() int {
616		if m.width < 20 {
617			return 80
618		}
619		return m.width
620	}
621	
622	func (m resumeTUI) renderHeader() string {
623		left := styBold.Render("resume") + styDim.Render(" · ") + m.projectName
624		agent := "all"
625		if m.agentFilter != "" {
626			agent = m.agentName(m.agentFilter)
627		}
628		scope := "[This project]"
629		if m.inBrowser {
630			scope = "[All projects ▸ " + m.projectName + "]"
631		}
632		right := styDim.Render("scope: ") + stySel.Render(scope) +
633			styDim.Render("   agent: ") + stySel.Render(agent)
634		gap := m.lineWidth() - lipgloss.Width(left) - lipgloss.Width(right)
635		if gap < 1 {
636			gap = 1
637		}
638		return left + strings.Repeat(" ", gap) + right
639	}
640	
641	func (m resumeTUI) renderRows() string {
642		if len(m.filtered) == 0 {
643			return styFaint.Render("  No sessions match.")
644		}
645		h := m.listHeight()
646		end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
647		var b strings.Builder
648		for i := m.top; i < end; i++ {
649			b.WriteString(m.sessionRow(m.filtered[i], i == m.cursor, m.snippetAt(i)))
650			if i < end-1 {
651				b.WriteString("\n")
652			}
653		}
654		return b.String()
655	}
656	
657	// snippetAt returns the search snippet for filtered row i, or "" (no active search).
658	func (m resumeTUI) snippetAt(i int) string {
659		if i >= 0 && i < len(m.filtered) {
660			s := m.filtered[i]
661			return m.filteredSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
662		}
663		return ""
664	}
665	
666	// sessionRow renders one list row. When snippet is non-empty (search active) the row
667	// shows the highlighted match context instead of the session title.
668	func (m resumeTUI) sessionRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
669		cursor := "  "
670		if selected {
671			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
672		}
673		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
674		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
675	
676		if m.viewMode == "sparse" {
677			turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d prompts", s.UserTurns))
678			label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-24)
679			if snippet != "" {
680				label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-26)
681			}
682			head := cursor + agent + "  " + label + "   " + turns
683			sub := "    " + styFaint.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%s ago · %s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt), shortID(s.SessionID)))
684			return head + "\n" + sub
685		}
686		turns := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%4d", s.UserTurns))
687		// A year-stamped date ("Dec 31 '25") is wider than the 4-col slot; shrink the label by the
688		// overflow so the right-hand turns column can't get pushed off the line and wrap.
689		extra := len(when) - 4
690		if extra < 0 {
691			extra = 0
692		}
693		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-22-extra)
694		if snippet != "" {
695			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-24-extra)
696		}
697		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + label + "  " + turns
698	}
699	
700	// renderSnippet renders a FTS snippet (matched terms wrapped in the control-char marks)
701	// with the matches highlighted, collapsing whitespace and clipping to maxWidth columns.
702	func renderSnippet(snip string, maxWidth int) string {
703		if maxWidth < 8 {
704			maxWidth = 8
705		}
706		snip = strings.NewReplacer("\n", " ", "\t", " ", "\r", " ").Replace(snip)
707		hl := lipgloss.NewStyle().Bold(true).Foreground(lipgloss.Color("232")).Background(lipgloss.Color("221"))
708	
709		var b strings.Builder
710		var seg strings.Builder
711		matched := false
712		visible := 0
713		flush := func() {
714			if seg.Len() == 0 {
715				return
716			}
717			if matched {
718				b.WriteString(hl.Render(seg.String()))
719			} else {
720				b.WriteString(seg.String())
721			}
722			seg.Reset()
723		}
724		for _, r := range snip {
725			switch r {
726			case '\x02':
727				flush()
728				matched = true
729				continue
730			case '\x03':
731				flush()
732				matched = false
733				continue
734			}
735			if visible >= maxWidth {
736				flush()
737				b.WriteString("…")
738				return b.String()
739			}
740			seg.WriteRune(r)
741			visible++
742		}
743		flush()
744		return b.String()
745	}
746	
747	func renderName(name string, selected bool, width int) string {
748		if width < 8 {
749			width = 8
750		}
751		t := truncate(name, width)
752		if selected {
753			return stySel.Render(t)
754		}
755		return t
756	}
757	
758	func (m resumeTUI) renderGlance() string {
759		sel := m.selected()
760		if sel == nil {
761			return ""
762		}
763		return styDim.Render("⟶  ") + styFaint.Render(truncate(sessionTitle(*sel), m.lineWidth()-4))
764	}
765	
766	func (m resumeTUI) renderFooter() string {
767		if m.searching {
768			return m.search.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc clear · enter apply")
769		}
770		scopeKey := "tab all-projects"
771		if m.inBrowser {
772			scopeKey = "tab/esc back"
773		}
774		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "↵ resume", "space preview", "/ search", "a agent", scopeKey, "v " + m.viewMode, "q quit"}
775		return styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, "   "))
776	}
777	
778	func (m resumeTUI) renderPreview() string {
779		var b strings.Builder
780		left := styBold.Render("Preview")
781		if s := m.readerSession; s != nil {
782			left += styDim.Render(" · ") + m.agentTag(s.Agent) +
783				styDim.Render(" · ") + sessionTitle(*s)
784		}
785		b.WriteString(left)
786		b.WriteString("\n")
787		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
788		b.WriteString("\n")
789		b.WriteString(m.reader.View())
790		b.WriteString("\n")
791		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()))
792		b.WriteString("\n")
793		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join([]string{"↑↓ scroll", "pgup/pgdn page", "space/esc close"}, " · ")))
794		return b.String()
795	}
796	
797	func (m resumeTUI) renderTarget() string {
798		var b strings.Builder
799		b.WriteString(styBold.Render("Resume into which agent?"))
800		if m.chosen != nil {
801			b.WriteString(styDim.Render("   " + sessionTitle(*m.chosen)))
802		}
803		b.WriteString("\n\n")
804		for i, a := range m.installed {
805			cursor := "   "
806			label := a.provider.Name()
807			if m.chosen != nil && a.id == m.chosen.Agent {
808				label += styFaint.Render(" (same agent — native resume)")
809			}
810			if i == m.targetCursor {
811				cursor = styCursor.Render(" ▸ ")
812				label = stySel.Render(a.provider.Name()) + label[len(a.provider.Name()):]
813			}
814			b.WriteString(cursor + label + "\n")
815		}
816		b.WriteString("\n")
817		b.WriteString(styDim.Render("↑↓ move   ↵ resume   esc back"))
818		return b.String()
819	}
820	
821	// ---- helpers ----
822	
823	func (m resumeTUI) agentName(id string) string {
824		if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
825			return a.name
826		}
827		return id
828	}
829	
830	func (m resumeTUI) agentTag(id string) string {
831		label := fmt.Sprintf("%-8s", id)
832		if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
833			return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(a.accent).Render(label)
834		}
835		return label
836	}
837	
838	// sessionTitle is the human label for a session: name, then slug, then short id.
839	func sessionTitle(s sessionindex.Session) string {
840		switch {
841		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Name) != "":
842			return s.Name
843		case strings.TrimSpace(s.Slug) != "":
844			return s.Slug
845		default:
846			return shortID(s.SessionID)
847		}
848	}
849	
850	// truncate shortens s to n runes with an ellipsis.
851	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
852		r := []rune(s)
853		if len(r) <= n {
854			return s
855		}
856		if n < 1 {
857			return ""
858		}
859		return string(r[:n-1]) + "…"
860	}
861	
862	// relativeTime renders an ISO 8601 timestamp as a compact "2m"/"3h"/"5d"/"Jun 2".
863	func relativeTime(iso string) string {
864		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
865		if err != nil {
866			return ""
867		}
868		d := time.Since(t)
869		switch {
870		case d < time.Minute:
871			return "now"
872		case d < time.Hour:
873			return fmt.Sprintf("%dm", int(d.Minutes()))
874		case d < 24*time.Hour:
875			return fmt.Sprintf("%dh", int(d.Hours()))
876		case d < 7*24*time.Hour:
877			return fmt.Sprintf("%dd", int(d.Hours()/24))
878		default:
879			lt := t.Local()
880			if lt.Year() == time.Now().Year() {
881				return lt.Format("Jan 2")
882			}
883			// Disambiguate prior years so "Dec 31" can't be mistaken for this year: "Dec 31 '25".
884			return lt.Format("Jan 2 '06")
885		}
886	}
887	
888	// ---- integration with the resume command ----
889	
890	// openOrBuildResumeIndex opens sessions.db, building it first (with the normal reindex
891	// UI) when it is missing or empty, then proceeding straight into the picker.
892	func openOrBuildResumeIndex() (*sessionindex.Store, error) {
893		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
894		if err != nil {
895			return nil, err
896		}
897		if _, statErr := os.Stat(dbPath); statErr == nil {
898			s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
899			if err != nil {
900				return nil, err
901			}
902			if n, _ := s.Count(); n > 0 {
903				return s, nil
904			}
905			_ = s.Close() // empty → rebuild below
906		}
907		if err := runReindex(false); err != nil {
908			return nil, err
909		}
910		return sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
911	}
912	
913	// selectResumeViaTUI runs the picker for the current project and returns the chosen
914	// resume plan (or nil if the user cancelled). On a successful selection it persists the
915	// view-mode and target-agent preferences to the user config.
916	func selectResumeViaTUI(registry *factory.Registry, store *sessionindex.Store, projectID, projectName, presetTo string) (*resumePlan, error) {
917		sessions, err := store.ListByProject(projectID)
918		if err != nil {
919			return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading sessions: %w", err)
920		}
921		// An empty current project is fine — the picker opens in the all-projects browser.
922		// Only bail when the whole index is empty (nothing to resume anywhere).
923		if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
924			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent here, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
925			return nil, nil
926		}
927	
928		agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
929		var installed []agentChoice
930		for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
931			prov, err := registry.Get(id)
932			if err != nil {
933				continue
934			}
935			agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
936			if prov.Check("").Success {
937				installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
938			}
939		}
940		if len(installed) == 0 {
941			fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo installed agents found to resume into.")
942			return nil, nil
943		}
944	
945		viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
946		if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
947			viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
948			lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
949		}
950	
951		model := newResumeTUI(store, registry, projectID, projectName, sessions, agents, installed, presetTo, lastAgent, viewMode)
952		final, err := tea.NewProgram(model).Run()
953		if err != nil {
954			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resume picker failed: %w", err)
955		}
956		rm := final.(resumeTUI)
957		if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
958			return nil, nil
959		}
960	
961		fromID := rm.result.session.Agent
962		fromProv, err := registry.Get(fromID)
963		if err != nil {
964			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", fromID, err)
965		}
966		toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
967		if err != nil {
968			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
969		}
970	
971		if err := config.SaveResumePrefs(rm.viewMode, rm.result.targetID); err != nil {
972			slog.Debug("resume: could not save prefs", "error", err)
973		}
974	
975		return &resumePlan{
976			from:      fromProv,
977			fromID:    fromID,
978			sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
979			fromCwd:   rm.result.session.OriginCwd,
980			to:        toProv,
981			toID:      rm.result.targetID,
982		}, nil
983	}
984	
985	// colorForAgent returns a stable accent color per provider for the list.
986	func colorForAgent(id string) color.Color {
987		switch id {
988		case "claude":
989			return lipgloss.Color("170") // purple
990		case "codex":
991			return lipgloss.Color("42") // green
992		case "cursor":
993			return lipgloss.Color("39") // blue
994		case "gemini":
995			return lipgloss.Color("45") // cyan
996		case "droid":
997			return lipgloss.Color("214") // orange
998		case "deepseek":
999			return lipgloss.Color("203") // red
1000		default:
1001			return lipgloss.Color("250")
1002		}
1003	}
1004	
1005	// ---- all-projects browser (Stage B) ----
1006	
1007	// enterBrowser switches to the all-projects browser, loading the project rollup lazily.
1008	func (m *resumeTUI) enterBrowser() {
1009		if !m.projectsLoaded {
1010			if ps, err := m.store.ListProjects(); err == nil {
1011				m.projects = ps
1012			}
1013			m.projectsLoaded = true
1014		}
1015		m.applyProjectFilter()
1016		m.mode = modeProjects
1017	}
1018	
1019	// gotoHome returns the session list to the current directory's project.
1020	func (m *resumeTUI) gotoHome() {
1021		m.projectID, m.projectName = m.homeProjectID, m.homeProjectName
1022		m.all = m.homeSessions
1023		m.inBrowser = false
1024		m.agentFilter, m.searchQuery = "", ""
1025		m.search.SetValue("")
1026		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
1027		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
1028		m.applyFilter()
1029		m.mode = modeList
1030	}
1031	
1032	// drillInto opens a project's session list from the browser.
1033	func (m *resumeTUI) drillInto(p sessionindex.ProjectSummary) {
1034		sessions, err := m.store.ListByProject(p.ProjectID)
1035		if err != nil {
1036			return
1037		}
1038		m.projectID = p.ProjectID
1039		m.projectName = projectDisplayName(p)
1040		m.all = sessions
1041		m.inBrowser = true
1042		m.agentFilter, m.searchQuery = "", ""
1043		m.search.SetValue("")
1044		m.cursor, m.top = 0, 0
1045		m.rebuildAgentCycle()
1046		m.applyFilter()
1047		m.mode = modeList
1048	}
1049	
1050	func (m resumeTUI) updateProjects(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1051		switch msg.String() {
1052		case "q", "ctrl+c":
1053			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1054			return m, tea.Quit
1055		case "esc":
1056			if m.startedInBrowser {
1057				m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1058				return m, tea.Quit
1059			}
1060			m.gotoHome()
1061		case "tab":
1062			if !m.startedInBrowser {
1063				m.gotoHome()
1064			}
1065		case "up", "k":
1066			m.moveProjCursor(-1)
1067		case "down", "j":
1068			m.moveProjCursor(1)
1069		case "pgup":
1070			m.moveProjCursor(-m.projectsHeight())
1071		case "pgdown":
1072			m.moveProjCursor(m.projectsHeight())
1073		case "home", "g":
1074			m.projCursor, m.projTop = 0, 0
1075		case "end", "G":
1076			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1077			m.clampProjScroll()
1078		case "enter":
1079			if m.projCursor >= 0 && m.projCursor < len(m.projFiltered) {
1080				m.drillInto(m.projFiltered[m.projCursor])
1081			}
1082		case "/":
1083			// FTS over sessions across ALL projects (consistent with / in a session list).
1084			m.globalActive = true
1085			m.globalSearching = true
1086			m.globalInput.SetValue(m.globalQuery)
1087			return m, m.globalInput.Focus()
1088		case "p":
1089			// Filter the project list by name.
1090			m.projSearching = true
1091			m.projSearch.SetValue(m.projSearchQuery)
1092			return m, m.projSearch.Focus()
1093		}
1094		return m, nil
1095	}
1096	
1097	func (m resumeTUI) updateProjectSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1098		switch msg.String() {
1099		case "esc":
1100			m.projSearching = false
1101			m.projSearch.Blur()
1102			m.projSearchQuery = ""
1103			m.applyProjectFilter()
1104			return m, nil
1105		case "enter":
1106			m.projSearching = false
1107			m.projSearch.Blur()
1108			return m, nil
1109		}
1110		var cmd tea.Cmd
1111		m.projSearch, cmd = m.projSearch.Update(msg)
1112		m.projSearchQuery = m.projSearch.Value()
1113		m.applyProjectFilter()
1114		return m, cmd
1115	}
1116	
1117	func (m *resumeTUI) applyProjectFilter() {
1118		q := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(m.projSearchQuery))
1119		if q == "" {
1120			m.projFiltered = m.projects
1121		} else {
1122			out := make([]sessionindex.ProjectSummary, 0, len(m.projects))
1123			for _, p := range m.projects {
1124				if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(projectDisplayName(p)), q) {
1125					out = append(out, p)
1126				}
1127			}
1128			m.projFiltered = out
1129		}
1130		if m.projCursor > len(m.projFiltered)-1 {
1131			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1132		}
1133		if m.projCursor < 0 {
1134			m.projCursor = 0
1135		}
1136		m.projTop = 0
1137	}
1138	
1139	func (m *resumeTUI) moveProjCursor(delta int) {
1140		if len(m.projFiltered) == 0 {
1141			return
1142		}
1143		m.projCursor += delta
1144		if m.projCursor < 0 {
1145			m.projCursor = 0
1146		}
1147		if m.projCursor > len(m.projFiltered)-1 {
1148			m.projCursor = len(m.projFiltered) - 1
1149		}
1150		m.clampProjScroll()
1151	}
1152	
1153	func (m *resumeTUI) clampProjScroll() {
1154		h := m.projectsHeight()
1155		if m.projCursor < m.projTop {
1156			m.projTop = m.projCursor
1157		}
1158		if m.projCursor >= m.projTop+h {
1159			m.projTop = m.projCursor - h + 1
1160		}
1161		if m.projTop < 0 {
1162			m.projTop = 0
1163		}
1164	}
1165	
1166	// projectsHeight reserves room for chrome plus a few date-bucket header lines.
1167	func (m resumeTUI) projectsHeight() int {
1168		const chrome = 9
1169		avail := m.height - chrome
1170		if avail < 1 {
1171			avail = 1
1172		}
1173		return avail
1174	}
1175	
1176	func (m resumeTUI) renderProjects() string {
1177		var b strings.Builder
1178	
1179		left := styBold.Render("resume") + styDim.Render(" · ") + "all projects"
1180		right := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d projects", len(m.projFiltered)))
1181		gap := m.lineWidth() - lipgloss.Width(left) - lipgloss.Width(right)
1182		if gap < 1 {
1183			gap = 1
1184		}
1185		b.WriteString(left + strings.Repeat(" ", gap) + right + "\n")
1186		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1187	
1188		if len(m.projFiltered) == 0 {
1189			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  No projects match."))
1190		} else {
1191			h := m.projectsHeight()
1192			end := min(m.projTop+h, len(m.projFiltered))
1193			lastBucket := ""
1194			for i := m.projTop; i < end; i++ {
1195				p := m.projFiltered[i]
1196				if bucket := dateBucket(p.LastActivity); bucket != lastBucket {
1197					b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  ── "+bucket) + "\n")
1198					lastBucket = bucket
1199				}
1200				b.WriteString(m.projectRow(p, i == m.projCursor) + "\n")
1201			}
1202		}
1203	
1204		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1205		if m.projSearching {
1206			b.WriteString(m.projSearch.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc clear · enter apply"))
1207			return b.String()
1208		}
1209		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "↵ open", "/ search sessions", "p filter projects"}
1210		if !m.startedInBrowser {
1211			keys = append(keys, "tab this project")
1212		}
1213		keys = append(keys, "q quit")
1214		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, "   ")))
1215		return b.String()
1216	}
1217	
1218	func (m resumeTUI) projectRow(p sessionindex.ProjectSummary, selected bool) string {
1219		cursor := "  "
1220		if selected {
1221			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
1222		}
1223		chips := m.agentCountChips(p.AgentCounts)
1224		when := relativeTime(p.LastActivity)
1225		return cursor + renderName(projectDisplayName(p), selected, 32) + "  " +
1226			chips + styDim.Render("  · "+when)
1227	}
1228	
1229	// agentCountChips renders "claude 12 · codex 3" with colored agent tags.
1230	func (m resumeTUI) agentCountChips(counts map[string]int) string {
1231		ids := make([]string, 0, len(counts))
1232		for id := range counts {
1233			ids = append(ids, id)
1234		}
1235		sort.Strings(ids)
1236		parts := make([]string, 0, len(ids))
1237		for _, id := range ids {
1238			tag := id
1239			if a, ok := m.agents[id]; ok {
1240				tag = lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(a.accent).Render(id)
1241			}
1242			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", tag, counts[id]))
1243		}
1244		return strings.Join(parts, styDim.Render(" · "))
1245	}
1246	
1247	func projectDisplayName(p sessionindex.ProjectSummary) string {
1248		if strings.TrimSpace(p.ProjectName) != "" {
1249			return p.ProjectName
1250		}
1251		if p.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || p.ProjectID == "" {
1252			return "(unknown project)"
1253		}
1254		return p.ProjectID
1255	}
1256	
1257	// dateBucket groups a timestamp into a relative bucket for the browser rollup.
1258	func dateBucket(iso string) string {
1259		t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, iso)
1260		if err != nil {
1261			return "Older"
1262		}
1263		switch d := dayDiff(t.Local()); {
1264		case d <= 0:
1265			return "Today"
1266		case d == 1:
1267			return "Yesterday"
1268		case d < 7:
1269			return "Previous 7 days"
1270		case d < 30:
1271			return "Previous 30 days"
1272		default:
1273			return "Older"
1274		}
1275	}
1276	
1277	// dayDiff returns whole calendar days between now and t (both local), now - t.
1278	func dayDiff(t time.Time) int {
1279		now := time.Now()
1280		ny, nm, nd := now.Date()
1281		ty, tm, td := t.Date()
1282		a := time.Date(ny, nm, nd, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1283		c := time.Date(ty, tm, td, 0, 0, 0, 0, now.Location())
1284		return int(a.Sub(c).Hours() / 24)
1285	}
1286	
1287	// ---- global session search (FTS across all projects, from the browser) ----
1288	
1289	func (m resumeTUI) updateGlobalSearch(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1290		switch msg.String() {
1291		case "esc":
1292			m.searchSeq++
1293			m.exitGlobal()
1294			return m, nil
1295		case "enter":
1296			m.globalSearching = false // commit → browse the results
1297			m.globalInput.Blur()
1298			return m, nil
1299		}
1300		var cmd tea.Cmd
1301		m.globalInput, cmd = m.globalInput.Update(msg)
1302		m.globalQuery = m.globalInput.Value()
1303		m.searchSeq++
1304		if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) {
1305			// Too short to search the whole corpus: no results until the 2nd character.
1306			m.globalResults = nil
1307			m.globalSnippets = nil
1308			m.snippetSeq++
1309			return m, cmd
1310		}
1311		return m, tea.Batch(cmd, searchDebounce(m.searchSeq, modeProjects))
1312	}
1313	
1314	func (m resumeTUI) updateGlobalResults(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1315		switch msg.String() {
1316		case "q", "ctrl+c":
1317			m.result = resumeTUIResult{cancelled: true}
1318			return m, tea.Quit
1319		case "esc":
1320			m.exitGlobal()
1321		case "/":
1322			m.globalSearching = true
1323			m.globalInput.SetValue(m.globalQuery)
1324			return m, m.globalInput.Focus()
1325		case "up", "k":
1326			m.moveGlobalCursor(-1)
1327			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1328		case "down", "j":
1329			m.moveGlobalCursor(1)
1330			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1331		case "pgup":
1332			m.moveGlobalCursor(-m.globalHeight())
1333			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1334		case "pgdown":
1335			m.moveGlobalCursor(m.globalHeight())
1336			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1337		case "home", "g":
1338			m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1339			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1340		case "end", "G":
1341			m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1342			m.clampGlobalScroll()
1343			return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(modeProjects)
1344		case "enter":
1345			// A global hit jumps straight to the target-agent step (fast path).
1346			if m.globalCursor >= 0 && m.globalCursor < len(m.globalResults) {
1347				m.chosen = &m.globalResults[m.globalCursor]
1348				m.mode = modeTarget
1349				m.targetCursor = m.defaultTargetIndex()
1350			}
1351		}
1352		return m, nil
1353	}
1354	
1355	func (m *resumeTUI) exitGlobal() {
1356		m.globalActive = false
1357		m.globalSearching = false
1358		m.globalInput.Blur()
1359		m.globalInput.SetValue("")
1360		m.globalQuery = ""
1361		m.globalResults = nil
1362		m.globalSnippets = nil
1363		m.snippetSeq++
1364		m.globalCursor, m.globalTop = 0, 0
1365	}
1366	
1367	func (m *resumeTUI) moveGlobalCursor(delta int) {
1368		if len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1369			return
1370		}
1371		m.globalCursor += delta
1372		if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1373			m.globalCursor = 0
1374		}
1375		if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1376			m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1377		}
1378		m.clampGlobalScroll()
1379	}
1380	
1381	func (m *resumeTUI) clampGlobalScroll() {
1382		h := m.globalHeight()
1383		if m.globalCursor < m.globalTop {
1384			m.globalTop = m.globalCursor
1385		}
1386		if m.globalCursor >= m.globalTop+h {
1387			m.globalTop = m.globalCursor - h + 1
1388		}
1389		if m.globalTop < 0 {
1390			m.globalTop = 0
1391		}
1392	}
1393	
1394	func (m resumeTUI) globalHeight() int {
1395		const chrome = 6
1396		avail := m.height - chrome
1397		if avail < 1 {
1398			avail = 1
1399		}
1400		return avail
1401	}
1402	
1403	func (m resumeTUI) renderGlobalResults() string {
1404		var b strings.Builder
1405	
1406		left := styBold.Render("resume") + styDim.Render(" · search all projects")
1407		if q := strings.TrimSpace(m.globalQuery); q != "" && !m.globalSearching {
1408			left += styDim.Render(" · ") + stySel.Render(q)
1409		}
1410		right := styDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d matches", len(m.globalResults)))
1411		gap := m.lineWidth() - lipgloss.Width(left) - lipgloss.Width(right)
1412		if gap < 1 {
1413			gap = 1
1414		}
1415		b.WriteString(left + strings.Repeat(" ", gap) + right + "\n")
1416		b.WriteString(strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1417	
1418		switch {
1419		case strings.TrimSpace(m.globalQuery) == "":
1420			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  Type to search sessions across all projects."))
1421		case len(m.globalResults) == 0:
1422			b.WriteString(styFaint.Render("  No matches."))
1423		default:
1424			h := m.globalHeight()
1425			end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1426			for i := m.globalTop; i < end; i++ {
1427				s := m.globalResults[i]
1428				snip := m.globalSnippets[sessionindex.FingerprintKey(s.Agent, s.SessionID)]
1429				b.WriteString(m.globalRow(s, i == m.globalCursor, snip))
1430				if i < end-1 {
1431					b.WriteString("\n")
1432				}
1433			}
1434		}
1435	
1436		b.WriteString("\n" + strings.Repeat("─", m.lineWidth()) + "\n")
1437		if m.globalSearching {
1438			b.WriteString(m.globalInput.View() + "    " + styFaint.Render("esc cancel · enter browse results"))
1439			return b.String()
1440		}
1441		keys := []string{"↑↓ move", "↵ resume", "/ edit search", "esc back", "q quit"}
1442		b.WriteString(styDim.Render(strings.Join(keys, "   ")))
1443		return b.String()
1444	}
1445	
1446	// globalRow renders a cross-project search hit: agent · time · project · highlighted
1447	// match snippet (global search always has a query, so a snippet is expected).
1448	func (m resumeTUI) globalRow(s sessionindex.Session, selected bool, snippet string) string {
1449		cursor := "  "
1450		if selected {
1451			cursor = styCursor.Render("▸ ")
1452		}
1453		agent := m.agentTag(s.Agent)
1454		when := fmt.Sprintf("%-4s", relativeTime(s.UpdatedAt))
1455		proj := fmt.Sprintf("%-18s", truncate(sessionProject(s), 18))
1456		label := renderName(sessionTitle(s), selected, m.lineWidth()-46)
1457		if snippet != "" {
1458			label = renderSnippet(snippet, m.lineWidth()-48)
1459		}
1460		return cursor + agent + " " + styDim.Render(when) + "  " + styFaint.Render(proj) + "  " + label
1461	}
1462	
1463	func sessionProject(s sessionindex.Session) string {
1464		if strings.TrimSpace(s.ProjectName) != "" {
1465			return s.ProjectName
1466		}
1467		if s.ProjectID == unknownProjectID || s.ProjectID == "" {
1468			return "(unknown)"
1469		}
1470		return s.ProjectID
1471	}
1472	
1473	// ---- async, debounced full-text search ----
1474	
1475	// searchDebounceMsg fires after a brief typing pause; the query runs only if its seq is
1476	// still current. searchResultMsg carries the async FTS results back to the model. Both
1477	// keep typing instant: the input updates synchronously while the query runs off-thread.
1478	type searchDebounceMsg struct {
1479		seq  int
1480		kind tuiMode // modeList (project-scoped) or modeProjects (global)
1481	}
1482	
1483	type searchResultMsg struct {
1484		seq      int
1485		kind     tuiMode
1486		sessions []sessionindex.Session
1487	}
1488	
1489	type resumeSnippetResultMsg struct {
1490		seq      int
1491		kind     tuiMode
1492		snippets map[string]string
1493	}
1494	
1495	const searchDebounceDelay = 50 * time.Millisecond
1496	
1497	// searchDebounce schedules a debounce tick. On fire, the model checks the seq and decides
1498	// whether to actually run the query (so only the last keystroke in a burst queries).
1499	func searchDebounce(seq int, kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1500		return tea.Tick(searchDebounceDelay, func(time.Time) tea.Msg {
1501			return searchDebounceMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1502		})
1503	}
1504	
1505	// runSearch returns a command that performs the FTS query off the UI thread.
1506	func (m resumeTUI) runSearch(seq int, kind tuiMode, ctx context.Context) tea.Cmd {
1507		store := m.store
1508		query, projectID := m.searchQuery, m.projectID
1509		if kind == modeProjects {
1510			query, projectID = m.globalQuery, ""
1511		}
1512		fq := ftsQuery(query)
1513		return func() tea.Msg {
1514			if !queryReady(query) {
1515				return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind}
1516			}
1517			sessions, _ := store.SearchContext(ctx, fq, projectID)
1518			return searchResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, sessions: sessions}
1519		}
1520	}
1521	
1522	// applySearchResults installs async FTS results into the matching list (scoped or global).
1523	func (m *resumeTUI) applySearchResults(kind tuiMode, sessions []sessionindex.Session) {
1524		if kind == modeProjects {
1525			m.globalResults = nil
1526			m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
1527			m.globalResults = append(m.globalResults, sessions...)
1528			if m.globalCursor > len(m.globalResults)-1 {
1529				m.globalCursor = len(m.globalResults) - 1
1530			}
1531			if m.globalCursor < 0 {
1532				m.globalCursor = 0
1533			}
1534			m.globalTop = 0
1535			return
1536		}
1537	
1538		var out []sessionindex.Session
1539		for _, s := range sessions {
1540			if m.agentFilter == "" || s.Agent == m.agentFilter {
1541				out = append(out, s)
1542			}
1543		}
1544		m.filtered = out
1545		m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
1546		if m.cursor > len(m.filtered)-1 {
1547			m.cursor = len(m.filtered) - 1
1548		}
1549		if m.cursor < 0 {
1550			m.cursor = 0
1551		}
1552		m.top = 0
1553		m.clampScroll()
1554	}
1555	
1556	func (m *resumeTUI) requestVisibleSnippets(kind tuiMode) tea.Cmd {
1557		var query string
1558		var rows []sessionindex.Session
1559		if kind == modeProjects {
1560			if !queryReady(m.globalQuery) || len(m.globalResults) == 0 {
1561				return nil
1562			}
1563			h := m.globalHeight()
1564			end := min(m.globalTop+h, len(m.globalResults))
1565			if m.globalTop >= end {
1566				return nil
1567			}
1568			query = m.globalQuery
1569			rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.globalResults[m.globalTop:end]...)
1570		} else {
1571			if !queryReady(m.searchQuery) || len(m.filtered) == 0 {
1572				return nil
1573			}
1574			h := m.listHeight()
1575			end := min(m.top+h, len(m.filtered))
1576			if m.top >= end {
1577				return nil
1578			}
1579			query = m.searchQuery
1580			rows = append([]sessionindex.Session(nil), m.filtered[m.top:end]...)
1581		}
1582	
1583		store := m.store
1584		fq := ftsQuery(query)
1585		m.snippetSeq++
1586		seq := m.snippetSeq
1587		return func() tea.Msg {
1588			snips, _ := store.SnippetsContext(context.Background(), fq, rows)
1589			return resumeSnippetResultMsg{seq: seq, kind: kind, snippets: snips}
1590		}
1591	}
1592
```

 `./pkg/cmd/search.go`

```text
30			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
31			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
32				registry := factory.GetRegistry()
33				initialQuery := strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(args, " "))
34	
35				cwd, err := os.Getwd()
36				if err != nil {
37					return err
38				}
39	
40				store, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
41				if err != nil {
42					return err
43				}
44				defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
45	
46				if total, _ := store.Count(); total == 0 {
47					fprintln(os.Stderr, "\nNo agent sessions indexed yet. Run an agent, then try again (or `specstory reindex`).")
48					return nil
49				}
50	
51				homeID, homeName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
52				if idErr != nil || homeID == "" {
53					homeID, homeName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
54				}
55				homeSessions, _ := store.ListByProject(homeID)
56				homeHasSessions := len(homeSessions) > 0
57	
58				agents := map[string]agentMeta{}
59				var installed []agentChoice
60				for _, id := range registry.ListIDs() {
61					prov, err := registry.Get(id)
62					if err != nil {
63						continue
64					}
65					agents[id] = agentMeta{name: prov.Name(), accent: colorForAgent(id)}
66					if prov.Check("").Success {
67						installed = append(installed, agentChoice{id: id, provider: prov})
68					}
69				}
70	
71				viewMode, lastAgent := "dense", ""
72				if cfg, _ := config.Load(nil); cfg != nil {
73					viewMode = cfg.GetResumeViewMode()
74					lastAgent = cfg.GetResumeLastAgent()
75				}
76	
77				model := newSearchTUI(store, registry, agents, installed, homeID, homeName, homeHasSessions, "", lastAgent, initialQuery)
78				final, err := tea.NewProgram(model).Run()
79				if err != nil {
80					return fmt.Errorf("search failed: %w", err)
81				}
82				rm := final.(searchTUI)
83				if rm.result.cancelled || rm.result.session == nil {
84					return nil // read-only session: nothing to launch
85				}
86	
87				// The user asked to resume a found session — launch via the shared path.
88				fromProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.session.Agent)
89				if err != nil {
90					return fmt.Errorf("unknown source agent %q: %w", rm.result.session.Agent, err)
91				}
92				toProv, err := registry.Get(rm.result.targetID)
93				if err != nil {
94					return fmt.Errorf("unknown target agent %q: %w", rm.result.targetID, err)
95				}
96				_ = config.SaveResumePrefs(viewMode, rm.result.targetID)
97	
98				plan := &resumePlan{
99					from:      fromProv,
100					fromID:    rm.result.session.Agent,
101					sessionID: rm.result.session.SessionID,
102					to:        toProv,
103					toID:      rm.result.targetID,
104				}
105				return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{useUTC: true})
106			},
107		}
108	}
109
```
