```text
1	# `specstory search` — find & read your sessions
2	
3	> **STATUS: BUILT (v1).** A read-first search TUI over the [`sessions.db` index](SESSIONS-DB.md),
4	> sibling to [`specstory resume`](RESUME-TUI.md) — shared UI, keymap vocabulary, and code
5	> (`launchResume`, `renderSnippet`, styles). The reader uses **glamour**. See
6	> [Build plan](#build-plan-built) for what shipped and what's deferred.
7	
8	## Purpose
9	
10	`specstory resume` answers *"continue a session"* — its end state is **launching an agent**.
11	`specstory search` answers *"find and read across my history"* — its end state is **reading
12	the session**. Same index, same look and feel; different terminal action.
13	
14	- **Read-first.** You search, you open a result, you read it. Viewing is the destination.
15	- **Resume is one keystroke away.** `r` on a result (or in the reader) hands off to the resume
16	  flow, so "I found the session I want to continue" is frictionless.
17	- **Pre-seeded query.** Anything after the command becomes the initial query and the search
18	  runs immediately — `specstory search max cpu` is exactly `specstory search` then typing
19	  `max cpu`. (Mirrors `specstory resume <agent>` pre-selecting the target.)
20	
21	## Relationship to `specstory resume`
22	
23	Consistency is the whole point — learnings and patterns must transfer both ways.
24	
25	**Shared (reuse, don't reinvent):**
26	
27	- The `sessions.db` index and its queries (`Search`, lazy `Snippets`, `ListByProject`,
28	  `ListProjects`, `SessionBody`).
29	- The Bubble Tea v2 model patterns, lipgloss styles, agent color tags, relative-time
30	  formatting, snippet highlighting (`renderSnippet`), and the **async + debounced FTS** path
31	  (`searchDebounceMsg`/`searchResultMsg`). These live in `pkg/cmd` already; the search TUI
32	  reuses them directly (same package).
33	- Result-row layout (`agent · time · project · highlighted snippet`) and keymap vocabulary:
34	  `↑↓`/`jk` move, `↵` primary action, `esc`/`q` back/quit, `a` agent filter, `tab` scope,
35	  `v` dense/sparse.
36	
37	**Different:**
38	
39	| | `resume` | `search` |
40	|---|---|---|
41	| Opens to | the current project's session list | **the search input** (you're here to search) |
42	| Default scope | current project, else all-projects; `tab` toggles | **all projects**; `tab` narrows to this project |
43	| `↵` does | → target-agent step → launch | → **open the reader** (read the session) |
44	| `r` | n/a | → resume this session (hands to the resume flow) |
45	| End state | agent running | session on screen |
46	
47	Scope **diverges deliberately**: search opens across **all projects** (it's a
48	find-across-history tool — you want the whole corpus first), and `tab` narrows to the
49	current project when it has indexed sessions. Resume does the opposite (current project
50	first). The always-on search input is the other intentional divergence.
51	
52	## UX
53	
54	Search input is always active at the top (it's a search tool), results below, a reader on
55	`↵`. Rough layout:
56	
57	```
58	 search · all projects                                   42 results   ·   agent: all
59	 / max cpu▌
60	 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
61	 ▸ claude   2d   specstory-cli   …diagnose the …max cpu… spike when reindex runs…
62	   codex    1w   intent-server   …the worker pegs …max… …cpu… at 100% during…
63	   gemini   3w   stoa-cli        …cap …cpu… usage; the …max… concurrency was…
64	 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	 ↑↓ move   ↵ read   r resume   a agent   tab this project   v dense/sparse   esc quit
66	```
67	
68	The **reader** (`↵`) — a full-screen, scrollable view of the session transcript with matches
69	highlighted and jumpable:
70	
71	```
72	 specstory-cli · claude · 2d                                              match 2 / 7
73	 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	   user ⟶  diagnose the [max cpu] spike when reindex runs on a big index
75	   agent ⟶ Let me profile… the snippet generation over hundreds of matches…
76	   …
77	 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78	 ↑↓ scroll   n/N next/prev match   r resume   esc back   q quit
79	```
80	
81	## Command
82	
83	`specstory search [query…]` — `cobra.ArbitraryArgs`; the args (joined with spaces) are the
84	initial query. No args → empty input, ready to type. Reuses `openOrBuildResumeIndex()`
85	(reindex if the index is missing/empty) before opening.
86	
87	## Decisions
88	
89	- **Scope defaults to all projects (ratified).** Search opens across the whole corpus and
90	  `tab` narrows to the current project (when it has sessions) — a deliberate divergence from
91	  resume, which defaults to the current project. Search is a find-across-history tool.
92	- **Query performance (ratified, built).** Full-text search only fires at **2+ characters**
93	  (a 1-char prefix like `t*` matches nearly the whole corpus); the main query returns session
94	  rows newest-first **without** `snippet()`, then fetches highlighted snippets lazily for only
95	  the visible rows. The browse path opens a **multi-connection reader pool** so a slow query
96	  can't freeze the UI, and each keystroke **cancels** the prior in-flight query (via `context`),
97	  freeing its connection. The results pane shows **`Searching…`** while a query is in flight
98	  rather than a premature "No matches". This lives in the shared FTS layer (`pkg/sessionindex`,
99	  `queryReady`), so resume gets it too.
100	- **`r` reuses resume's full target-agent flow (ratified).** Cross-agent power + one consistent
101	  resume path, rather than a separate same-agent shortcut.
102	- **Always-on search input (ratified).** The input is focused at the top from the start; type to
103	  search live. This is the one intentional divergence from resume's `/`-to-search.
104	- **Reader = glamour (ratified, built).** The reader renders the session as markdown
105	  (`session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession`) through `glamour` for a rich "pretty" read, in a
106	  scrollable `viewport`. Falls back to the plain FTS body for sessions that can't be re-parsed
107	  (no resolvable cwd, e.g. Cursor). In-reader match highlighting + `n`/`N` nav is deferred
108	  (glamour emits its own ANSI; needs a post-process pass or a plain match-view toggle).
109	
110	## Build plan **(built)**
111	
112	1. **`pkg/cmd/search.go`** — the `specstory search [query…]` command; args pre-seed the query;
113	   reuses `openOrBuildResumeIndex`; launches the TUI; on `r`, builds a `resumePlan` and calls
114	   the shared `launchResume`.
115	2. **`pkg/cmd/search_tui.go`** — Bubble Tea model: always-on input, async-debounced
116	   cross-project results (reusing `renderSnippet`, styles, `renderAgentTag`, `sessionTitle`,
117	   relative time, the FTS path), `tab` scope toggle, `ctrl+a` agent filter, glamour reader.
118	3. **Reader** — `bubbles/viewport` over **glamour-rendered** session markdown
119	   (`session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession` → `glamour`; falls back to the FTS body for
120	   sessions that can't be re-parsed, e.g. Cursor). Scrollable; `r` resumes.
121	4. **`r` → resume** — `launchResume` factored out of `resume.go` and shared; `r` (reader) /
122	   `ctrl+r` (results) → target-agent step → reconstruct + `ExecAgentAndWatch`.
123	
124	**Keybinding adaptation (the always-on-input consequence).** Because the input is always
125	focused, bare letters type into the query, so secondary actions use non-letter / `ctrl`
126	bindings, and `r` lives where there's no input:
127	
128	- Results: type to search · `↑↓`/`ctrl+p`/`ctrl+n` move · `↵` **read** · `ctrl+r` resume ·
129	  `tab` scope · `ctrl+a` agent · `esc` clear-then-quit.
130	- Reader: `↑↓`/`pgup`/`pgdn` scroll · `r` resume · `esc` back · `q` quit.
131	
132	**Dependency note.** glamour (`github.com/charmbracelet/glamour` v1.0.0) is still on the old
133	charm stack; it collides with our v2 `x/ansi` until `x/cellbuf` is pinned to **v0.0.15** (done
134	in go.mod). Main build stays green.
135	
136	**Deferred:** in-reader match highlighting + `n`/`N` navigation (glamour emits its own ANSI, so
137	this needs a post-process pass or a plain "match view" toggle); dense/sparse in results (rows
138	are always snippet rows); a shared-component extraction pass if the duplication with
139	`resume_tui.go` grows. Interactive UX validated by running in a terminal.
140	
141	## Out of scope
142	
143	- Index population / freshness — see [SESSIONS-DB.md](SESSIONS-DB.md). `search` joins `resume`
144	  as a staleness-refresh occasion in the warm-keeping thread.
145	- Cross-machine / cloud search — later stages of session portability.
146	
147	## Related Documents
148	
149	- [RESUME-TUI.md](RESUME-TUI.md) — the sibling picker whose UI/keymap/code this shares.
150	- [SESSIONS-DB.md](SESSIONS-DB.md) — the index both read from.
151
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# `specstory resume` — the picker TUI
2	
3	The interactive session picker that makes SpecStory the best way to resume a coding-agent
4	session. It reads the [`sessions.db` index](SESSIONS-DB.md) and launches the chosen session
5	via the existing reconstruct + `ExecAgentAndWatch` plumbing (see
6	[SESSION-PORTABILITY.md](SESSION-PORTABILITY.md)). This doc covers the UX and the build plan;
7	it replaces the old plain numbered-menu selection in `pkg/cmd/resume.go`.
8	
9	## Decisions (ratified)
10	
11	- **TUI stack:** Bubble Tea v2 + Bubbles v2 + Lipgloss v2 (the `charm.land/*/v2` modules —
12	  latest, and aligned with the `lipgloss/v2` already pulled in by `fang`). Not the v1 stack
13	  stoa-cli pins; we use the latest.
14	- **No source-agent step.** The picker shows **all sessions across all agents** for the
15	  current project by default, each row tagged with its agent. (The old flow's "pick the source
16	  agent first" is gone.)
17	- **Empty current project → all-projects view.** If the current project has no sessions, skip
18	  the project list and open directly in the all-projects view.
19	- **Empty/missing `sessions.db` → reindex first.** If the index doesn't exist, run `reindex`
20	  (with its normal progress UI) and then continue straight into the picker (don't exit).
21	- **Dense / sparse view modes.** Dense = more sessions, less per-session detail; sparse = more
22	  detail, fewer sessions. Toggle is easy/obvious; the choice is **remembered** in
23	  `~/.specstory/cli/config.toml` `[resume] view_mode` (via `config.SaveResumePrefs`).
24	- **Preview pane** shows the session: first user message · truncated middle · final message,
25	  sized to fit. Sourced from the stored FTS body (`Store.SessionBody`); Cursor/metadata-only
26	  sessions fall back to name/slug.
27	- **All-projects view rolls up by relative date** (Today · Yesterday · Previous 7 days ·
28	  Previous 30 days · Older) by each project's latest activity, showing per-agent session counts
29	  (`Store.ListProjects`); the user expands a project to see its sessions.
30	- **Target agent (last step).** `specstory resume` lets the user pick the target agent as the
31	  final step; `specstory resume <agent>` pre-selects it. The **last-resumed agent** is the
32	  enter-default, remembered in `[resume] last_agent`.
33	- **`/` is always session full-text search; only its scope changes.** In a session list `/`
34	  is FTS scoped to that project; in the all-projects browser `/` is FTS across *all* projects
35	  (a flat results list, project shown per row, `↵` jumps straight to the target-agent step).
36	  Project-name filtering is a *separate* key, **`p`** (browser only) — never a single box that
37	  mode-switches between the two.
38	- **Search results show the match, not the title.** Each result row renders the FTS
39	  `snippet()` (matched terms highlighted) in place of the session title once the visible-row
40	  snippet fetch lands, so you see *why* it matched without making the main search pay for every
41	  match. The query runs **async + debounced** (`searchDebounceMsg`/`searchResultMsg`, ~50ms)
42	  off the UI thread, `LIMIT`-bounded, newest-first, and snippet-free; snippets are fetched
43	  lazily for the visible window.
44	
45	## Data foundation (built)
46	
47	- `config`: `[resume]` section (`view_mode`, `last_agent`), `GetResumeViewMode()` /
48	  `GetResumeLastAgent()`, and `SaveResumePrefs()` — a section-preserving writer that upserts
49	  only `[resume]` so the self-documenting template's comments survive.
50	- `sessionindex`: `ListByProject(projectID)` (sessions, newest first), `ListProjects()`
51	  (date-sortable rollup with per-agent counts), `SessionBody(agent, sessionID)` (preview),
52	  `Search(query)` (global FTS). All tested.
53	
54	## Build plan
55	
56	### Stage A — current-project picker **(built)**
57	
58	`pkg/cmd/resume_tui.go` — a Bubble Tea v2 model wired into `resume.go`:
59	
60	- Mixed-agent session list for the current project (`ComputeProjectID(cwd)` →
61	  `ListByProject`), newest first, colored agent tags.
62	- Agent filter (`a` cycles all → each present agent); dense/sparse toggle (`v`, persisted
63	  via `SaveResumePrefs`); preview overlay (`space`, first/middle/last from `SessionBody`);
64	  full-text search (`/` → FTS, scoped to the project).
65	- Missing/empty `sessions.db` → `reindex` (normal progress UI) then continue.
66	- Select a session (`↵`) → target-agent step (pre-selected by `resume <agent>`; else the
67	  last-resumed agent; else the session's own agent) → hands off to the existing
68	  `prepareResumeTarget` + `ExecAgentAndWatch`.
69	- Keys: `↑↓`/`jk` move · `↵` resume · `space` preview · `/` search · `a` agent · `v`
70	  dense/sparse · `q`/`esc` quit.
71	
72	**Deferred to Stage B / follow-up:** empty current project currently shows a message rather
73	than jumping to all-projects (that view *is* Stage B); the `tab` scope toggle; and persisting
74	the view-mode on cancel (today it saves only on a committed resume). The interactive UX itself
75	is validated by running it in a real terminal (it can't be exercised headless).
76	
77	### Stage B — all-projects browser **(built)**
78	
79	- A `modeProjects` screen: the `ListProjects` rollup grouped by relative date buckets
80	  (Today · Yesterday · Previous 7 days · Previous 30 days · Older) by each project's latest
81	  activity, each row showing the project name, colored per-agent count chips, and relative
82	  time. `↵` drills into a project's session list (the Stage A list, scoped); `esc`/`tab`
83	  returns to the browser.
84	- **Scope toggle:** `tab` from the home session list opens the browser; `tab` (or `esc`)
85	  from the browser returns to the current project.
86	- **Empty current project → browser:** the picker opens directly in `modeProjects`.
87	- **Search:** `/` in the browser runs **session FTS across all projects** → a flat results
88	  list (agent · time · project · title), `↵` jumps straight to the target-agent step, `esc`
89	  back to the rollup. `p` filters the **project list by name**. (`/` in a drilled-in session
90	  list stays project-scoped FTS — same key, scope follows the view.)
91	- Header reflects scope: `[This project]` vs `[All projects ▸ <name>]`. The whole-index
92	  empty case (nothing indexed at all) prints a hint instead of opening an empty browser.
93	
94	## Out of scope (here)
95	
96	- Reconstruction / launch plumbing — unchanged (`prepareResumeTarget`, `ExecAgentAndWatch`).
97	- Index population / freshness — see [SESSIONS-DB.md](SESSIONS-DB.md). `resume` is one of the
98	  staleness-trigger occasions, handled in the warm-keeping thread.
99
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
# `specstory search` — find & resume your sessions

> **STATUS: BUILT.** `search` is the **same interactive TUI as [`resume`](RESUME-TUI.md)**
> (`sessionTUI` in `pkg/cmd/resume_tui.go`), entered straight into the all-projects full-text
> search over the [`sessions.db` index](SESSIONS-DB.md). There is no separate search model —
> the two commands share one codebase, keymap, and look. The preview uses **glamour**.

## Purpose

`specstory search` answers *"find something across all my history."* It opens directly in the
cross-project full-text search with the input focused, so you type immediately. From a hit you
**preview** it (`space`) or **resume** it (`r`) — the same preview and resume flow as `resume`.

- **Pre-seeded query.** Anything after the command becomes the initial query and the search
  runs immediately — `specstory search max cpu` is exactly `specstory search` then typing
  `max cpu`. (Mirrors `specstory resume <agent>` pre-selecting the target.)

## Relationship to `specstory resume`

They are **one UI, two doors**. The only differences:

| | `resume [agent]` | `search [query]` |
|---|---|---|
| Opens to | the current project's session list | the all-projects FTS, **input focused** |
| Positional arg | pre-selects the target agent | pre-seeds the query |
| Header title | `SpecStory Resume` | `SpecStory Search` |

Everything else is shared *because it is literally the same model* (`newSessionTUI` +
`sessionTUIOpts`): keymap, the glamour preview, agent filter, dense/sparse, snippet
highlighting, the async + debounced FTS path, and the target-agent step. `search` reaches the
cross-project results via the model's existing global-search screen (`globalActive`); `resume`
reaches the same screen with `tab` → `/`.

## UX

The search input is the footer line (bottom), results above, consistent with `resume`'s
`/`-search. Rough layout:

\```
 SpecStory Search · all projects                              42 matches   ·   agent: all
 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ▸ claude   2d   specstory-cli   …diagnose the …max cpu… spike when reindex runs…
   codex    1w   intent-server   …the worker pegs …max… …cpu… at 100% during…
   gemini   3w   stoa-cli        …cap …cpu… usage; the …max… concurrency was…
 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ↑↓ move · r resume · space preview · a agent · v dense · / edit search · esc back · q quit
 / max cpu▌
\```

`space` opens a scrollable, **glamour-rendered** reader of the session (the real specstory
markdown via `session.GenerateMarkdownFromAgentSession`, falling back to the plain FTS body for
sessions that can't be re-parsed, e.g. Cursor). `r` (in the list or the preview) hands off to
the shared target-agent step → reconstruct + `ExecAgentAndWatch`. `↵` is inert in the list, so
a stray return can't accidentally launch an agent.

## Command

`specstory search [query…]` — `cobra.ArbitraryArgs`; the args (joined with spaces) are the
initial query. No args → empty input, ready to type. Reuses `openOrBuildResumeIndex()`
(reindex if the index is missing/empty) before opening the shared TUI with
`sessionTUIOpts{startInSearch: true}`.

## Decisions

- **One model, two entry points (ratified, built).** `search` was originally a separate
  always-on-input model (`search_tui.go`); it has been folded into `sessionTUI`. This removed
  the `ctrl`-key bindings that the always-on input forced, and gave `search` the agent filter,
  dense/sparse, and glamour preview for free. `search_tui.go` is deleted.
- **Query performance (ratified, built).** Full-text search only fires at **2+ characters**
  (a 1-char prefix like `t*` matches nearly the whole corpus); the main query returns session
  rows newest-first **without** `snippet()`, then fetches highlighted snippets lazily for only
  the visible rows. Each keystroke **cancels** the prior in-flight query (via `context`),
  freeing its connection. Lives in the shared FTS layer (`pkg/sessionindex`, `queryReady`).
- **Resume = `r`, not `↵` (ratified, built).** Launching an agent is an explicit keystroke in
  every list; `↵` only commits on the final target-agent confirmation screen.
- **Preview = glamour (ratified, built).** The session renders as markdown through `glamour` in
  a scrollable `viewport`, shared verbatim with `resume`. In-preview match highlighting +
  `n`/`N` nav is deferred (glamour emits its own ANSI; needs a post-process pass).

## Out of scope

- Index population / freshness — see [SESSIONS-DB.md](SESSIONS-DB.md). `search` joins `resume`
  as a staleness-refresh occasion in the warm-keeping thread.
- Cross-machine / cloud search — later stages of session portability.

Done. `search` and `resume` are now one UI behind one model.

## What changed

**One model, two doors.** `resumeTUI` → **`sessionTUI`** (`pkg/cmd/resume_tui.go`) now backs both commands. `search_tui.go` (~660 lines) is **deleted**. The only per-command differences live in `sessionTUIOpts`:

| | `resume [agent]` | `search [query]` |
|---|---|---|
| Opens to | this project's session list | all-projects FTS, **input focused** |
| Positional arg | pre-selects target agent | pre-seeds the query |
| Title | `SpecStory Resume` | `SpecStory Search` |

`search` reaches the cross-project results through the model's *existing* global-search screen, so it inherited agent filter, dense/sparse, and the glamour preview for free — and you still type immediately because it starts focused in the input.

**Your normalization list, all applied (both UIs):**
- Title is `SpecStory Resume` / `SpecStory Search`.
- Header scope reads **`project: getspecstory`** (name in white) — or `all projects`.
- **`space` preview** everywhere (the glamour reader).
- **`r` resume, `enter` inert** in the lists — no accidental launches. `↵` only commits on the final target-agent confirm screen.
- **Dot delimiters** in every footer.
- Search input sits at the **bottom**.
- **No `ctl-` cuteness** — it's modal, so single keys (`q` quits, `ctrl+c` always hard-quits). The old `^r`/`^a` are gone.
- **Dense/sparse (`v`)** now works in search.

I also fixed a latent bug: resuming a cross-project hit from `search` now passes `fromCwd` (origin cwd), so the source loads from the right directory.

**Verification:** `go build`, `golangci-lint` (0 issues), and `go test ./...` all pass. Docs updated (`RESUME-TUI.md`, `SESSION-SEARCH.md`).

**One thing I couldn't do here:** the TUI needs a real TTY, so I couldn't drive it interactively in this sandbox. Please run `./specstory search` and `./specstory resume` to eyeball it. Two spots worth a specific look:
- `search` → type → `space` preview → `esc` → `r` resume (the preview-as-overlay-over-global-results path is new).
- `a`/`v` inside `search` results (agent filter + dense/sparse on the cross-project list).

One deliberate call to confirm: I used bare **`space preview`** in footers (matching the other bare keys like `r resume`) rather than literal `<space> preview`. Say the word if you want the angle brackets.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 13:19:37Z)**_

The bug is in `ftsQuery`. When you type `"thank you"`, it strips the quotes and every non-alphanumeric char, turning each word into an independent prefix term: `thank* you*`. FTS5 then ANDs those two prefixes and matches them **anywhere, in any order** — and `you*` is a prefix, so it also matches `your`, `youtube`, `yourself`. That's exactly the partial-match mess in your screenshot.

There's no phrase handling at all today — the quotes never reach the query. Let me check for existing tests, then fix it.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 13:20:06Z)**_

The query string goes straight into FTS5 `MATCH`, and `ftsQuery` only ever emits tokens it builds itself — so whatever syntax I generate is safe (no raw user punctuation reaches MATCH). Now the fix.

Here's the design for the corrected `ftsQuery`:

- **Bare words** (no quotes): unchanged — `thank you` → `thank* you*` (loose AND of prefixes; standard search).
- **Closed quoted phrase**: `"thank you"` → `"thank you"` — a real FTS5 phrase: tokens **adjacent, in order**, no prefix. This is the fix.
- **Open quote** (still mid-typing): `"thank yo` → `thank + yo*` — phrase adjacency with a prefix on the final partial word, so results keep updating live as you type.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 13:21:46Z)**_

This confirms the fix behaves exactly as designed against real FTS5:

- **`thank* you*`** (bare words, unchanged) → rows 1, 2, 4, 5 — the loose, match-anywhere behavior you saw in the screenshot. Row 3 (`youtube` alone, no `thank`) correctly excluded by the AND.
- **`"thank you"`** (closed phrase) → **only row 1** — exact adjacency. This is the fix: `thank your`, `youtube`, `thank them` no longer match.
- **`thank + you*`** (open phrase, mid-typing) → rows 1 and 2 — adjacency preserved (`you should thank` doesn't match), with the trailing prefix still letting `thank your` through so type-ahead stays live until you close the quote.

## Summary

The bug: `ftsQuery` stripped the quotes and turned `"thank you"` into `thank* you*` — two independent prefix terms AND-ed anywhere, so `your`/`youtube`/separated `thank`…`you` all lit up. Quotes never carried phrase meaning.

The fix (`pkg/cmd/session_tui.go`):
- Bare words → `word*` prefix AND (unchanged loose search).
- **Closed quotes → a real FTS5 phrase** `"thank you"`: adjacent, in order, no prefix bleed.
- Open quote (still typing) → `thank + you*`: phrase adjacency with a live prefix on the final word.

Tokenization was factored into a small `alnumWords` helper so `ftsQuery` and `queryReady` agree on what a token is. Added `TestFTSQuery` (11 cases) and `TestQueryReady` to the existing `resume_test.go` rather than a new file. Build, lint (0 issues), and the full `pkg/cmd` suite all pass.

One thing worth your awareness: this is **opt-in phrase search** — you have to type the quotes. Unquoted multi-word search stays loose (match-anywhere AND), which is the conventional default. If you'd rather quotes *also* drop the prefix entirely even mid-type, or want some other default, say so — but I'd recommend leaving it as is.

OK, let's talk about keeping the index warm... right now the only thing indexing is `specstory reindex`. We want to keep it warm as a side-effect of other operations too.

So, let's start w/ the most straight forward one... `specstory resume` and `specstory search`

Let's have those do 2 things, both in the "background".

First: reindex the cwd... so get the session list for the cwd from all agents and update the index for the cwd, all silently in the background
Then: when that completes, do the full reindex that happens with `specstory reindex`, but just silently in the background

If the user quits the `resume` or `search` before the reindex finishes, that's fine, just kill it, we don't want to block the process exit.

And I believe already if session.db doesn't exist and you run `specstory resume` or `specstory search` then it will do a re-index first in the foreground. None of this is meant to replace that. Do verify that is the behavior.

Ask any questions.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 20:14:07Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>

```text
package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"log/slog"
	"os"
	"os/signal"
	"runtime"
	"strings"
	"sync"
	"sync/atomic"
	"syscall"
	"time"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"

	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/analytics"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/sessionindex"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/factory"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi/schema"
	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/utils"
)

const (
	// unknownProjectID buckets sessions whose originating cwd could not be resolved
	// (a few Claude warmups; all Cursor sessions until the deferred cwd recovery lands).
	unknownProjectID = "unknown"

	// reindexWriteBatch is how many sessions the writer goroutine commits per transaction.
	reindexWriteBatch = 200

	// reindexVersion is the logic version stamped on every indexed row. Bump it whenever
	// the parse/flatten/derivation logic changes so the next reindex re-parses everything
	// even when files are unchanged. It is part of the freshness fingerprint.
	//   2: skip slash-command noise when choosing a Claude session title (fixes UUID titles)
	//   3: also skip "[Request interrupted by user…]" markers
	//   4: canonicalize (case-fold) the workspace_id path hash + prefer a persisted
	//      .project.json workspace_id, so a project's id no longer varies with path casing
	reindexVersion = 4
)

// CreateReindexCommand builds the `specstory reindex` command: a full, from-scratch
// rebuild of the restore index (~/.specstory/sessions.db) of every coding-agent session
// SpecStory can find, across all projects and providers. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
func CreateReindexCommand() *cobra.Command {
	cmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "reindex",
		Short: "Rebuild the restore index of all known agent sessions",
		Long: `Rebuild the restore index used by 'specstory resume'.

'reindex' enumerates every session across all installed agents and projects and writes a
searchable index to ~/.specstory/sessions.db. It is incremental: a session whose native
file is unchanged since it was last indexed is skipped, so re-runs are fast. Use --force to
re-index everything regardless. The index is a derived cache: it is safe to delete.`,
		Args: cobra.NoArgs,
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			force, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("force")
			return runReindex(force)
		},
	}
	cmd.Flags().Bool("force", false, "re-index every session even if unchanged")
	return cmd
}

// reindexItem is one unit of work: a session to parse and index. size/mtime are the
// native file's stat, captured during the freshness check and reused (no re-stat).
type reindexItem struct {
	agent string
	prov  spi.Provider
	ref   spi.GlobalSessionRef
	size  int64
	mtime int64
}

func runReindex(force bool) error {
	start := time.Now()

	// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
	defer cancel()

	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
	}
	defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()

	registry := factory.GetRegistry()

	// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently ----
	fprintln(os.Stderr, "\n🔍  Scanning agents…")
	ids := registry.ListIDs()
	perProvider := make([][]spi.GlobalSessionRef, len(ids))
	provs := make([]spi.Provider, len(ids))
	var ewg sync.WaitGroup
	for i, id := range ids {
		prov, err := registry.Get(id)
		if err != nil {
			continue
		}
		provs[i] = prov
		ewg.Add(1)
		go func(i int, id string, prov spi.Provider) {
			defer ewg.Done()
			refs, err := prov.ListAllAgentChatSessions()
			if err != nil {
				slog.Warn("reindex: enumeration failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
			}
			perProvider[i] = refs
		}(i, id, prov)
	}
	ewg.Wait()

	// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
	fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
	if !force {
		if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
			slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
		} else {
			fingerprints = fps
		}
	}

	// Dedup enumerated refs by (agent, session_id), keeping the freshest file. A resumed
	// session can span MULTIPLE native files sharing one id; they all map to one
	// (agent, session_id) row, so without dedup the duplicates ping-pong — each run, one
	// file's stat never matches the fingerprint the other file just stored, re-indexing
	// both forever. Skipping the freshest by mtime makes the fingerprint stable. Sessions
	// with no native id are dropped (unresumable, and would collide on the PK).
	best := map[string]reindexItem{}
	var order []string // first-seen order, for deterministic processing
	for i, id := range ids {
		if provs[i] == nil {
			continue
		}
		for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
			if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
				slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
				continue
			}
			size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
			item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
			key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
			if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
				if mtime > cur.mtime {
					best[key] = item
				}
				continue
			}
			best[key] = item
			order = append(order, key)
		}
	}

	// Apply the incremental fingerprint skip to the deduped set.
	var work []reindexItem
	totals := map[string]int{}        // to-do (changed) per agent — drives the progress bars
	foundPerAgent := map[string]int{} // distinct sessions per agent — for the "Found" line
	found := 0
	unchanged := 0
	for _, key := range order {
		item := best[key]
		found++
		foundPerAgent[item.agent]++
		if fp, ok := fingerprints[key]; ok &&
			fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
			unchanged++
			continue // already indexed and unchanged
		}
		work = append(work, item)
		totals[item.agent]++
	}

	if found == 0 {
		fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
		return nil
	}
	fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
	if len(work) == 0 {
		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
		return nil
	}
	if unchanged > 0 {
		fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
	} else {
		fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
	}

	// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine) ----
	prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
	prog.begin()

	indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
	cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}

	sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
	var writeErr error
	writerDone := make(chan struct{})
	go func() {
		defer close(writerDone)
		writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
	}()

	workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
	workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
	var wwg sync.WaitGroup
	for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
		wwg.Add(1)
		go func() {
			defer wwg.Done()
			for item := range workCh {
				sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
				prog.observe(sess.ProjectID)
				sessionsCh <- sess
				prog.inc(item.agent)
			}
		}()
	}

	for _, item := range work {
		select {
		case <-ctx.Done():
			goto closing
		case workCh <- item:
		}
	}
closing:
	close(workCh)
	wwg.Wait()
	close(sessionsCh)
	<-writerDone
	prog.end()

	if writeErr != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
	}

	// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
	elapsed := time.Since(start)
	projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
	unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
	printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
		"sessions":     len(work),
		"unchanged":    unchanged,
		"projects":     projects,
		"unattributed": unattributed,
		"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
	})
	return nil
}

// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
// dropped.
func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
	ref := item.ref
	projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)

	sess := sessionindex.Session{
		ProjectID:    projectID,
		ProjectName:  projectName,
		Agent:        item.agent,
		SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
		CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,
		UpdatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt, // default; overwritten by last-turn timestamp below
		Slug:         ref.Slug,
		Name:         ref.Name,
		NativePath:   ref.NativePath,
		OriginCwd:    ref.OriginCwd,
		Size:         item.size, // captured during the freshness check (no re-stat)
		Mtime:        item.mtime,
		IndexVersion: reindexVersion,
		IndexedAt:    indexedAt,
	}

	if ref.OriginCwd == "" {
		return sess // cannot re-locate without a cwd → metadata-only
	}
	full, err := item.prov.GetAgentChatSession(ref.OriginCwd, ref.SessionID, false)
	if err != nil || full == nil || full.SessionData == nil {
		slog.Debug("reindex: full parse unavailable, indexing metadata only",
			"agent", item.agent, "session", ref.SessionID, "error", err)
		return sess
	}

	data := full.SessionData
	sess.Body = flattenBody(data)
	sess.UserTurns, sess.TotalTurns = countTurns(data)
	if ts := lastTimestamp(data); ts != "" {
		sess.UpdatedAt = ts
	}
	return sess
}

// drainToStore writes parsed sessions to the index in batched transactions.
func drainToStore(store *sessionindex.Store, ch <-chan sessionindex.Session) error {
	batch := make([]sessionindex.Session, 0, reindexWriteBatch)
	flush := func() error {
		if err := store.UpsertBatch(batch); err != nil {
			return err
		}
		batch = batch[:0]
		return nil
	}
	for sess := range ch {
		batch = append(batch, sess)
		if len(batch) >= reindexWriteBatch {
			if err := flush(); err != nil {
				return err
			}
		}
	}
	return flush()
}

// ---- session field derivation ----

// flattenBody renders SessionData to plain user/agent text for full-text indexing,
// reusing the reconstruction flattener (synthetic noise already stripped). No
// migration note is prepended — this is index content, not a resumed transcript.
func flattenBody(data *schema.SessionData) string {
	turns := spi.FlattenSessionData(data, "")
	var b strings.Builder
	for _, t := range turns {
		if t.Text == "" {
			continue
		}
		if b.Len() > 0 {
			b.WriteString("\n\n")
		}
		b.WriteString(t.Text)
	}
	return b.String()
}

// countTurns returns (user prompts, all messages) across the session's exchanges.
func countTurns(data *schema.SessionData) (userTurns, totalTurns int) {
	for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
		for _, m := range ex.Messages {
			totalTurns++
			if m.Role == schema.RoleUser {
				userTurns++
			}
		}
	}
	return userTurns, totalTurns
}

// lastTimestamp returns the last non-empty message timestamp in the session, or "".
func lastTimestamp(data *schema.SessionData) string {
	last := ""
	for _, ex := range data.Exchanges {
		for _, m := range ex.Messages {
			if m.Timestamp != "" {
				last = m.Timestamp
			}
		}
	}
	return last
}

// statNative returns the native file's size (bytes) and mtime (epoch ms), or zeros.
func statNative(path string) (size, mtimeMs int64) {
	info, err := os.Stat(path)
	if err != nil {
		return 0, 0
	}
	return info.Size(), info.ModTime().UnixMilli()
}

// ---- project identity cache (cwd -> project id/name) ----

type projectIDName struct{ id, name string }

// projectIDCache memoizes ComputeProjectID by cwd, since many sessions share a cwd and
// each resolution walks the filesystem to the git root.
type projectIDCache struct {
	mu sync.Mutex
	m  map[string]projectIDName
}

func (c *projectIDCache) resolve(cwd string) (string, string) {
	if strings.TrimSpace(cwd) == "" {
		return unknownProjectID, ""
	}
	c.mu.Lock()
	defer c.mu.Unlock()
	if v, ok := c.m[cwd]; ok {
		return v.id, v.name
	}
	id, name, err := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
	if err != nil || id == "" {
		id, name = unknownProjectID, ""
	}
	c.m[cwd] = projectIDName{id, name}
	return id, name
}

// ---- helpers ----

// summarizeCounts renders "claude 683 · codex 70 · …" in registry order, omitting zeros.
func summarizeCounts(ids []string, totals map[string]int) string {
	var parts []string
	for _, id := range ids {
		if totals[id] > 0 {
			parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s %d", id, totals[id]))
		}
	}
	return strings.Join(parts, " · ")
}

// ---- live progress (per-agent bars) ----

// reindexProgress renders live per-agent progress. Workers bump atomic per-agent
// counters and observe project ids; a single render goroutine redraws the block in
// place on a TTY (ANSI cursor-up), or prints periodic plain lines otherwise. No worker
// draws directly. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md "Reindex Progress UX".
type reindexProgress struct {
	agents []string          // agents with >0 sessions, in registry order
	totals map[string]int    // per-agent session totals
	done   map[string]*int64 // per-agent completed counters (atomic)
	tty    bool

	mu       sync.Mutex
	projects map[string]struct{} // distinct non-unknown project ids seen

	stopCh     chan struct{}
	doneCh     chan struct{}
	linesDrawn int
}

func newReindexProgress(ids []string, totals map[string]int) *reindexProgress {
	done := make(map[string]*int64)
	var agents []string
	for _, id := range ids {
		if totals[id] > 0 {
			agents = append(agents, id)
			done[id] = new(int64)
		}
	}
	return &reindexProgress{
		agents:   agents,
		totals:   totals,
		done:     done,
		tty:      isTerminal(os.Stderr),
		projects: map[string]struct{}{},
		stopCh:   make(chan struct{}),
		doneCh:   make(chan struct{}),
	}
}

func (p *reindexProgress) begin() {
	go p.loop()
}

func (p *reindexProgress) end() {
	close(p.stopCh)
	<-p.doneCh
	p.draw(true) // final frame at 100%
}

func (p *reindexProgress) inc(agent string) {
	if c := p.done[agent]; c != nil {
		atomic.AddInt64(c, 1)
	}
}

func (p *reindexProgress) observe(projectID string) {
	// Unknown-project sessions are not counted toward the live "projects" total (the
	// final unattributed count comes from the DB at summary time).
	if projectID == unknownProjectID {
		return
	}
	p.mu.Lock()
	p.projects[projectID] = struct{}{}
	p.mu.Unlock()
}

func (p *reindexProgress) projectCount() int {
	p.mu.Lock()
	defer p.mu.Unlock()
	return len(p.projects)
}

func (p *reindexProgress) totalDone() int {
	var n int64
	for _, c := range p.done {
		n += atomic.LoadInt64(c)
	}
	return int(n)
}

func (p *reindexProgress) grandTotal() int {
	n := 0
	for _, a := range p.agents {
		n += p.totals[a]
	}
	return n
}

func (p *reindexProgress) loop() {
	defer close(p.doneCh)
	interval := 100 * time.Millisecond
	if !p.tty {
		interval = 1500 * time.Millisecond
	}
	ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
	defer ticker.Stop()
	for {
		select {
		case <-p.stopCh:
			return
		case <-ticker.C:
			p.draw(false)
		}
	}
}

func (p *reindexProgress) draw(final bool) {
	if !p.tty {
		if !final { // the final state is covered by the summary line
			fprintf(os.Stderr, "indexed %d/%d…\n", p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal())
		}
		return
	}

	lines := p.frameLines()
	var b strings.Builder
	if p.linesDrawn > 0 {
		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "\033[%dA", p.linesDrawn) // move cursor up to the block's top
	}
	for _, ln := range lines {
		b.WriteString("\033[2K") // clear the line, then redraw it
		b.WriteString(ln)
		b.WriteString("\n")
	}
	fprint(os.Stderr, b.String())
	p.linesDrawn = len(lines)
}

func (p *reindexProgress) frameLines() []string {
	doneN, totalN := p.totalDone(), p.grandTotal()
	lines := []string{
		fmt.Sprintf("Indexing  %d/%d · %d projects", doneN, totalN, p.projectCount()),
		"",
	}

	nameW := 0
	for _, a := range p.agents {
		if len(a) > nameW {
			nameW = len(a)
		}
	}
	for _, a := range p.agents {
		d := atomic.LoadInt64(p.done[a])
		t := int64(p.totals[a])
		check := ""
		if d >= t {
			check = " ✓"
		}
		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("  %-*s %s %d/%d%s", nameW, a, progressBar(d, t), d, t, check))
	}
	return lines
}

// progressBar renders a fixed-width unicode bar, e.g. ▕███████░░░░░░░▏.
func progressBar(done, total int64) string {
	const w = 14
	filled := 0
	if total > 0 {
		filled = int(done * int64(w) / total)
	}
	if filled > w {
		filled = w
	}
	return "▕" + strings.Repeat("█", filled) + strings.Repeat("░", w-filled) + "▏"
}

// isTerminal reports whether f is a character device (an interactive terminal),
// using only the standard library (no isatty dependency).
func isTerminal(f *os.File) bool {
	info, err := f.Stat()
	if err != nil {
		return false
	}
	return info.Mode()&os.ModeCharDevice != 0
}

// ---- summary ----

func printReindexSummary(ids []string, perAgent map[string]int, dbPath string, elapsed time.Duration, indexed, unchanged, projects, unattributed int) {
	w := os.Stderr
	if unchanged > 0 {
		fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions (%d unchanged) into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, unchanged, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
	} else {
		fprintf(w, "\n✓   Indexed %d sessions into %s  (%.1fs)\n\n", indexed, prettyPath(dbPath), elapsed.Seconds())
	}
	fprintf(w, "      %s\n\n", summarizeCounts(ids, perAgent))
	if unattributed > 0 {
		fprintf(w, "      %d projects  ·  %d unattributed\n", projects, unattributed)
	} else {
		fprintf(w, "      %d projects\n", projects)
	}
	fprintf(w, "\n")
}

// prettyPath abbreviates the user's home directory to ~ for display.
func prettyPath(p string) string {
	if home, err := os.UserHomeDir(); err == nil && strings.HasPrefix(p, home) {
		return "~" + p[len(home):]
	}
	return p
}

```text
// reconstruct (cross-agent) or native-resume (same agent) and launch with auto-save.
func CreateResumeCommand(cloudURL *string, localTimeZone bool, debugDir string) *cobra.Command {
	longDesc := `Resume a past coding-agent session — in the same agent, or a different one.

'resume' opens an interactive picker of the sessions in the current project across all
agents. Pick one, choose which installed agent to continue it in, and go. Resuming into a
different agent reconstructs the conversation into that agent's native format first.

Pass an agent to pre-select the resume target, e.g. 'specstory resume codex'.`

	resumeCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "resume [agent]",
		Short: "Resume a past session, optionally in a different agent",
		Long:  longDesc,
		Args:  cobra.MaximumNArgs(1),
		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
			config.EnsureDefaultProjectConfig()
			slog.Info("Running in resume mode")

			registry := factory.GetRegistry()

			// Optional positional arg pre-selects the target agent (e.g. `resume codex`).
			presetTarget := ""
			if len(args) == 1 {
				presetTarget = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(args[0]))
			}

			// Read flags (subset mirroring run/watch).
			debugRaw, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("debug-raw")
			useLocalTimezone, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("local-time-zone")
			useUTC := !useLocalTimezone
			outputDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("output-dir")
			flagDebugDir, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("debug-dir")
			noCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-cloud-sync")
			onlyCloudSync, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("only-cloud-sync")
			provenanceEnabled, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("provenance")
			noTelemetryPrompts, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-telemetry-prompts")

			if flagDebugDir != "" {
				spi.SetDebugBaseDir(flagDebugDir)
			}

			cwd, err := os.Getwd()
			if err != nil {
				slog.Error("Failed to get current working directory", "error", err)
				return err
			}

			// Open (building if needed) the session index and run the interactive picker.
			store, err := openOrBuildResumeIndex()
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()

			projectID, projectName, idErr := utils.ComputeProjectID(cwd)
			if idErr != nil || projectID == "" {
				projectID, projectName = unknownProjectID, filepath.Base(cwd)
			}

			plan, err := selectResumeViaTUI(registry, store, projectID, projectName, presetTarget)
			if err != nil {
				return err
			}
			if plan == nil {
				// User quit or nothing to resume; the picker already explained why.
				return nil
			}

			return launchResume(plan, cwd, resumeLaunchOpts{
				outputDir:          outputDir,
				flagDebugDir:       flagDebugDir,
				debugRaw:           debugRaw,
				useUTC:             useUTC,
				noCloudSync:        noCloudSync,
				onlyCloudSync:      onlyCloudSync,
				provenanceEnabled:  provenanceEnabled,
				noTelemetryPrompts: noTelemetryPrompts,
			})
		},
	}

	resumeCmd.Flags().String("output-dir", "", "custom output directory for markdown files (default: ./.specstory/history)")
	resumeCmd.Flags().String("debug-dir", debugDir, "custom output directory for debug data (default: ./.specstory/debug)")
	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("only-cloud-sync", false, "skip local markdown file saves, only upload to cloud (requires authentication)")
	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-cloud-sync", false, "disable cloud sync functionality")
	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("debug-raw", false, "debug mode to output pretty-printed raw data files")
	_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("debug-raw")
	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("local-time-zone", localTimeZone, "use local timezone for file name and content timestamps (when not present: UTC)")
	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("provenance", false, "enable AI provenance tracking (correlate file changes to agent activity)")
	_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("provenance")
	resumeCmd.Flags().StringVar(cloudURL, "cloud-url", "", "override the default cloud API base URL")
	_ = resumeCmd.Flags().MarkHidden("cloud-url")
	resumeCmd.Flags().Bool("no-telemetry-prompts", false, "exclude prompt text from telemetry spans, if telemetry is enabled")

	return resumeCmd
}

// `search` (whose `r` action resumes a found session through the same path).

func openOrBuildResumeIndex() (*sessionindex.Store, error) {
	dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	if _, statErr := os.Stat(dbPath); statErr == nil {
		s, err := sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
		if err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
		if n, _ := s.Count(); n > 0 {
			return s, nil
		}
		_ = s.Close() // empty → rebuild below
	}
	if err := runReindex(false); err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}
	return sessionindex.OpenReader(dbPath)
}
=== ListAgentChatSessions usage / GlobalSessionRef shape ===
package spi

// GlobalSessionRef is a lightweight, project-discovering reference to a single
// native session, returned by Provider.ListAllAgentChatSessions.
//
// Unlike the project-scoped ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath), the project is NOT
// an input here: each ref carries the originating working directory (read from inside
// the session) so the caller — the `specstory reindex` command — can resolve project
// identity with utils.ComputeProjectID. The project is discovered, not supplied.
//
// It is intentionally metadata-only (no full SessionData parse); reindex re-fetches
// full data per ref via GetAgentChatSession(OriginCwd, SessionID) when it needs the
// conversation body. See docs/SESSIONS-DB.md.
type GlobalSessionRef struct {
	SessionID  string // native session id (uuid)
	CreatedAt  string // ISO 8601 creation timestamp (first turn), may be empty
	Slug       string // filename-safe slug derived from the first user message
	Name       string // human-readable description (may be empty)
	NativePath string // absolute path the provider opens to read this session
	OriginCwd  string // working directory the session was launched from (-> project_id)
}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 20:16:36Z)**_

I've now traced the whole path. Before I build this, let me confirm the architecture I found and surface the decisions that actually change the design.

**What I verified (your assumption is correct):** `openOrBuildResumeIndex()` (used by *both* `resume` and `search`) opens the index read-only; if the file is **missing or empty (0 rows)** it runs `runReindex(false)` in the **foreground** with the full progress UI, then proceeds. This warming work is additive and won't touch that path.

**What I found that shapes the implementation:**
- `runReindex` is one function that interleaves the indexing *engine* (enumerate → dedup → incremental fingerprint-skip → parse → write) with the **foreground progress UI** (progress bars + `os.Stderr` prints). I can't just call it in a goroutine — it would draw progress bars *over the Bubble Tea alt-screen* and corrupt the TUI. So I'll extract the silent engine and reuse it (DRY), leaving the foreground command behavior identical.
- The store is WAL-mode with a reader handle (TUI) + separate writer handle (background) — concurrent read+write is fine, so this is safe.
- Both your phases need a global enumeration anyway, so I'll enumerate **once** and run the cwd subset first, then the rest — same result you described, no double scan.
- On TUI exit I'll cancel the context and **return without waiting**; WAL makes an abandoned in-flight write batch safe, so the process exits immediately.

Two genuine decisions I want your call on:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-24 20:18:15Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**The open resume/search TUI loads its data at startup — before the background cwd warm finishes. Should that completed cwd pass refresh what's on screen, or is warming purely for future runs?**

Options:
- **Warm only, no live refresh** - Simplest. The background passes update the index on disk for the NEXT invocation. The currently-open list/results are not re-queried mid-session. Matches 'keep the index warm' literally.
- **Refresh list after cwd pass** - When the cwd pass finishes, re-query and update the resume list (and search results) in place so new/changed sessions for THIS project appear without restarting. More moving parts in the TUI; small risk of the list shifting under the cursor.

**When `resume`/`search` finds NO index and builds the whole thing in the foreground first, should the background warm still run for that invocation?**

Options:
- **Skip warm that run (Recommended)** - The foreground full build already made the index 100% fresh, so the background cwd+full passes would be redundant re-enumeration. Skip warming when we just built it; warm normally on all subsequent runs where the index already existed.
- **Always warm** - Run the background warm unconditionally, even right after a foreground build. Simpler/uniform code path; costs one redundant (but incremental, mostly no-op) enumeration that run.


**Answer:** Refresh list after cwd pass" selected preview:
resume opens → shows list (as of startup)
  ↳ bg: cwd reindex completes
     → list re-queries, new sessions appear
  ↳ bg: full reindex completes (silent), Skip warm that run (Recommended)" selected preview:
db missing → foreground full reindex (fresh)
           → skip background warm (redundant)

db exists → run TUI + background warm
</details></tool-use>

func (p *Program) Send(msg Msg) {
	select {
	case <-p.ctx.Done():
	case p.msgs <- msg:
	}
}

// Quit is a convenience function for quitting Bubble Tea programs. Use it
// when you need to shut down a Bubble Tea program from the outside.
//
// If you wish to quit from within a Bubble Tea program use the Quit command.
//
// If the program is not running this will be a no-op, so it's safe to call
// if the program is unstarted or has already exited.
func (p *Program) Quit() {
	p.Send(Quit())
}

// Kill stops the program immediately and restores the former terminal state.
// The final render that you would normally see when quitting will be skipped.
// [program.Run] returns a [ErrProgramKilled] error.
func (p *Program) Kill() {
	p.shutdown(true)
```

77	func runReindex(force bool) error {
78		start := time.Now()
79	
80		// Graceful Ctrl+C: stop feeding work, flush what's written, exit cleanly.
81		ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
82		defer cancel()
83	
84		dbPath, err := sessionindex.DefaultPath()
85		if err != nil {
86			return err
87		}
88		store, err := sessionindex.Open(dbPath)
89		if err != nil {
90			return fmt.Errorf("opening restore index: %w", err)
91		}
92		defer func() { _ = store.Close() }()
93	
94		registry := factory.GetRegistry()
95	
96		// ---- Phase 1: enumerate every provider concurrently ----
97		fprintln(os.Stderr, "\n🔍  Scanning agents…")
98		ids := registry.ListIDs()
99		perProvider := make([][]spi.GlobalSessionRef, len(ids))
100		provs := make([]spi.Provider, len(ids))
101		var ewg sync.WaitGroup
102		for i, id := range ids {
103			prov, err := registry.Get(id)
104			if err != nil {
105				continue
106			}
107			provs[i] = prov
108			ewg.Add(1)
109			go func(i int, id string, prov spi.Provider) {
110				defer ewg.Done()
111				refs, err := prov.ListAllAgentChatSessions()
112				if err != nil {
113					slog.Warn("reindex: enumeration failed", "provider", id, "error", err)
114				}
115				perProvider[i] = refs
116			}(i, id, prov)
117		}
118		ewg.Wait()
119	
120		// Existing fingerprints, so unchanged sessions can be skipped (the incremental path).
121		fingerprints := map[string]sessionindex.Fingerprint{}
122		if !force {
123			if fps, err := store.Fingerprints(); err != nil {
124				slog.Warn("reindex: could not load fingerprints, re-indexing all", "error", err)
125			} else {
126				fingerprints = fps
127			}
128		}
129	
130		// Dedup enumerated refs by (agent, session_id), keeping the freshest file. A resumed
131		// session can span MULTIPLE native files sharing one id; they all map to one
132		// (agent, session_id) row, so without dedup the duplicates ping-pong — each run, one
133		// file's stat never matches the fingerprint the other file just stored, re-indexing
134		// both forever. Skipping the freshest by mtime makes the fingerprint stable. Sessions
135		// with no native id are dropped (unresumable, and would collide on the PK).
136		best := map[string]reindexItem{}
137		var order []string // first-seen order, for deterministic processing
138		for i, id := range ids {
139			if provs[i] == nil {
140				continue
141			}
142			for _, ref := range perProvider[i] {
143				if strings.TrimSpace(ref.SessionID) == "" {
144					slog.Debug("reindex: skipping session with no id", "agent", id, "path", ref.NativePath)
145					continue
146				}
147				size, mtime := statNative(ref.NativePath)
148				item := reindexItem{agent: id, prov: provs[i], ref: ref, size: size, mtime: mtime}
149				key := sessionindex.FingerprintKey(id, ref.SessionID)
150				if cur, ok := best[key]; ok {
151					if mtime > cur.mtime {
152						best[key] = item
153					}
154					continue
155				}
156				best[key] = item
157				order = append(order, key)
158			}
159		}
160	
161		// Apply the incremental fingerprint skip to the deduped set.
162		var work []reindexItem
163		totals := map[string]int{}        // to-do (changed) per agent — drives the progress bars
164		foundPerAgent := map[string]int{} // distinct sessions per agent — for the "Found" line
165		found := 0
166		unchanged := 0
167		for _, key := range order {
168			item := best[key]
169			found++
170			foundPerAgent[item.agent]++
171			if fp, ok := fingerprints[key]; ok &&
172				fp.Size == item.size && fp.Mtime == item.mtime && fp.Version == reindexVersion {
173				unchanged++
174				continue // already indexed and unchanged
175			}
176			work = append(work, item)
177			totals[item.agent]++
178		}
179	
180		if found == 0 {
181			fprintln(os.Stderr, "No agent sessions found to index.")
182			return nil
183		}
184		fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   Found %d sessions  ·  %s\n", found, summarizeCounts(ids, foundPerAgent))
185		if len(work) == 0 {
186			fprintf(os.Stderr, "✓   All %d sessions already up to date.  (%.1fs)\n", found, time.Since(start).Seconds())
187			return nil
188		}
189		if unchanged > 0 {
190			fprintf(os.Stderr, "    %d unchanged · indexing %d…\n\n", unchanged, len(work))
191		} else {
192			fprintln(os.Stderr, "")
193		}
194	
195		// ---- Phase 2+3: parse (worker pool) → write (single goroutine) ----
196		prog := newReindexProgress(ids, totals)
197		prog.begin()
198	
199		indexedAt := start.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
200		cache := &projectIDCache{m: map[string]projectIDName{}}
201	
202		sessionsCh := make(chan sessionindex.Session, 256)
203		var writeErr error
204		writerDone := make(chan struct{})
205		go func() {
206			defer close(writerDone)
207			writeErr = drainToStore(store, sessionsCh)
208		}()
209	
210		workCh := make(chan reindexItem)
211		workers := min(runtime.NumCPU(), 12)
212		var wwg sync.WaitGroup
213		for w := 0; w < workers; w++ {
214			wwg.Add(1)
215			go func() {
216				defer wwg.Done()
217				for item := range workCh {
218					sess := buildSession(item, cache, indexedAt)
219					prog.observe(sess.ProjectID)
220					sessionsCh <- sess
221					prog.inc(item.agent)
222				}
223			}()
224		}
225	
226		for _, item := range work {
227			select {
228			case <-ctx.Done():
229				goto closing
230			case workCh <- item:
231			}
232		}
233	closing:
234		close(workCh)
235		wwg.Wait()
236		close(sessionsCh)
237		<-writerDone
238		prog.end()
239	
240		if writeErr != nil {
241			return fmt.Errorf("writing restore index: %w", writeErr)
242		}
243	
244		// ---- Summary (counts reflect the WHOLE index, not just this run's work) ----
245		elapsed := time.Since(start)
246		projects, _ := store.ProjectCount(unknownProjectID)
247		unattributed, _ := store.UnattributedCount(unknownProjectID)
248		printReindexSummary(ids, foundPerAgent, dbPath, elapsed, prog.totalDone(), unchanged, projects, unattributed)
249		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventReindexCompleted, analytics.Properties{
250			"sessions":     len(work),
251			"unchanged":    unchanged,
252			"projects":     projects,
253			"unattributed": unattributed,
254			"duration_ms":  elapsed.Milliseconds(),
255		})
256		return nil
257	}
258	
259	// buildSession turns one enumeration ref into a fully-built index row. Every session
260	// yields a row from its metadata; the body + turn counts + last-activity time are
261	// ENRICHED via a full parse only when the originating cwd lets us re-locate the
262	// session (so Cursor, whose cwd is unknown for now, lands metadata-only). Nothing is
263	// dropped.
264	func buildSession(item reindexItem, cache *projectIDCache, indexedAt string) sessionindex.Session {
265		ref := item.ref
266		projectID, projectName := cache.resolve(ref.OriginCwd)
267	
268		sess := sessionindex.Session{
269			ProjectID:    projectID,
270			ProjectName:  projectName,
271			Agent:        item.agent,
272			SessionID:    ref.SessionID,
273			CreatedAt:    ref.CreatedAt,

204	func (m sessionTUI) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
205		switch msg := msg.(type) {
206		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
207			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
208			m.reader.SetWidth(m.width)
209			m.reader.SetHeight(m.previewHeight())
210			return m, nil
211		case searchDebounceMsg:
212			// Fire the actual query only if no newer keystroke has arrived (debounce).
213			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
214				if m.searchCancel != nil {
215					m.searchCancel() // abort any prior in-flight query, freeing its connection
216				}
217				ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
218				m.searchCancel = cancel
219				return m, m.runSearch(msg.seq, msg.kind, ctx)
220			}
221			return m, nil
222		case searchResultMsg:
223			// Apply only the latest query's results (discard stale async results).
224			if msg.seq == m.searchSeq {
225				m.applySearchResults(msg.kind, msg.sessions)
226				return m, m.requestVisibleSnippets(msg.kind)
227			}
228			return m, nil
229		case snippetResultMsg:
230			if msg.seq == m.snippetSeq {
231				if msg.kind == modeProjects {
232					if m.globalSnippets == nil {
233						m.globalSnippets = map[string]string{}
234					}
235					for key, snip := range msg.snippets {
236						m.globalSnippets[key] = snip
237					}
238				} else {
239					if m.filteredSnippets == nil {
240						m.filteredSnippets = map[string]string{}
```

433	`UNINDEXED` is an FTS5 per-column keyword meaning the column is **stored but excluded from the
434	full-text index** — not "lacks a b-tree index" (FTS5 tables have no secondary b-tree indexes at
435	all). The two join keys ride along in the row so a `MATCH` hit maps straight back to its
436	`sessions` row without a separate lookup table. Default FTS5 tokenizer. Rows are
437	inserted/replaced alongside their `sessions` row during
438	`reindex`; deletion of a `sessions` row removes its `sessions_fts` row.
439	
440	## Non-Goals (first cut)
441	
442	- **The picker rewiring.** `specstory resume`'s interactive flow still uses the project-scoped
443	  `ListAgentChatSessions` until a later chunk points it at `sessions.db`.
444	- **Any warm-keeping.** No steady-state upserts from `sync`/`run`/`watch`/`resume` yet — the
445	  only population path is the explicit `reindex` (which is incremental, so re-runs are cheap).
446	- **Storing serialized `SessionData`.** The first cut stores metadata + full-text body, not a
447	  `SessionData` blob. A blob becomes relevant for cloud Stages 3–4 (where the cloud is the
448	  source). Deferred.
449	
450	## Deferred — DO NEXT (Cursor cwd recovery)
451	
452	Cursor CLI sessions are currently enumerated with `OriginCwd=""` and therefore indexed
453	under the **`unknown` project_id**. This is a first-cut shortcut, not the end state — it is
454	the immediate follow-up after the `reindex` first cut.
455	
456	Cursor stores sessions at `~/.cursor/chats/<projectHash>/<sessionID>/store.db`, where
457	`projectHash = md5(canonical project path)` (`cursorcli/path_utils.go` `GetProjectHashDir`),
458	and records no workspace path inside the store. md5 is one-way, but the hash is embedded in
459	`GlobalSessionRef.NativePath` (two directories up from `store.db`). **Recovery plan:** the
460	`reindex` orchestrator builds `map[md5(canonicalize(cwd))]cwd` from every *other* provider's
461	cwds (optionally augmented by a filesystem scan for git/`.specstory` roots), then matches each
462	Cursor ref's `projectHash` to recover its cwd → resolve `project_id` via `ComputeProjectID`.
463	Unmatched Cursor sessions remain `unknown`. (Tracked in memory: `restore-cursor-cwd-deferred`.)
464

All done and verified. Here's what shipped.

## Summary

`specstory resume` and `specstory search` now keep `sessions.db` warm in the background, exactly as you specified.

**Verified your existing-behavior assumption:** `openOrBuildResumeIndex()` (used by both commands) still builds the whole index in the **foreground** with full progress UI when the file is missing or has 0 rows. Untouched — and now it also reports `builtFresh` so we can skip redundant warming right after.

### What happens now on `resume`/`search`
While the picker is open, a background goroutine runs the **same incremental engine** as `specstory reindex`, silently, in two passes:
1. **Current project first** — fast; on completion it re-queries the open TUI (`indexWarmedMsg`) so new/changed sessions for this project appear in place (cursor preserved by session id), per your "refresh after cwd pass" choice.
2. **Full corpus** — silent, no UI refresh.

On TUI exit the warm is cancelled and the process exits immediately — never blocked. An abandoned write batch is safe under WAL. And when the index was just built fresh in the foreground, the warm is skipped (your "skip that run" choice).

### How it's built (DRY, no duplication)
I extracted `runReindex`'s engine into reusable pieces — `enumerateAll` → `dedupRefs` → `selectWork(projectFilter)` → `processWork(reporter)` — so the foreground command and the background warm share identical indexing logic. The **only** difference is the progress reporter: live bars (`*reindexProgress`) vs. silent (`nopReporter`). `selectWork` gained an optional project filter for the cwd pass; the warm enumerates once and reuses it for both passes (reloading fingerprints between them so pass-1's writes aren't re-done).

### Validation
- `reindex` (which now runs entirely through the extracted engine) works end-to-end: 812 sessions found, incremental skip correct, summary identical to before.
- `go build`, `golangci-lint` (0 issues), full `go test ./...` all pass.
- Docs updated: SESSIONS-DB.md's warm-keeping section now reflects shipped reality.

**What I could not exercise here:** the TUI live-refresh path (`indexWarmedMsg` → in-place list update) needs a real terminal. The plumbing compiles and the engine underneath is validated, but please confirm the on-screen behavior by running `specstory resume` in a project, creating a new agent session in another terminal, and watching the list pick it up after a moment. Same for `search`.

Two notes for your awareness:
- **No throttling yet** — every `resume`/`search` invocation triggers a fresh enumeration. It's cheap (incremental), but if you bounce in and out rapidly it re-scans each time. I flagged it as open in the docs; easy to add a "last warmed" timestamp later if it matters.
- **Background workers** are capped at `min(NumCPU, 4)` (vs. 12 foreground) to stay gentle while you're interacting.
